Extract deterministic_candidates from raw content (node type, title, fields) 3. `add_node_candidate(session_id, candidate)` — adds to in-memory session 4. `add_edge_candidate(session_id, candidate)` — adds relationships 5. `get_similar_nodes(session_id, candidate_id)` — checks for existing similar nodes 6. `propose_reconciliation(session_id)` — computes ADD/UPDATE/SUPERSEDE/NOOP hints 7. `commit_consolidation(session_id)` — atomic write to Kùzu ## Extension Steps for New Node Types 1. Add node type extraction logic in deterministic_candidates builder 2. Define CREATE NODE TABLE for global meta-graph schema 3. Map board-specific fields to global node properties 4. Define edge types for cross-node relationships 5. Update kg_query_global to include new types in search scope 6. Verify ACL enforcement filters correctly for new types ## Key Code Paths - okto_pulse.core.kg.consolidation: pipeline orchestration - okto_pulse.core.kg.global_meta_graph: schema and query layer - okto_pulse.core.kg.discovery: cross-board search", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "a5d1e7c4-a38f-4a52-b074-d2c8469f8293", "title": "Global Discovery Query Layer", "description": "[propagated from parent] How global discovery queries work and what needs updating for new node types", "content": "# Global Discovery Query Layer ## Current Implementation - `kg_query_global(board_id, nl_query, top_k)` — cross-board semantic search - Returns matching decisions from all boards agent has access to - ACL filtering applied automatically based on board membership ## Query Flow 1. Embed the natural language query (sentence-transformers or stub) 2. Search global meta-graph using HNSW index (or title-prefix match fallback) 3. Filter results by board ACL 4. Return top_k results with similarity scores ## Extension Requirements - New node types must be included in the search scope - Each node type may need custom embedding strategy (e.g., Bug nodes embed severity + description) - Discovery queries should support filtering by node type - Consider adding node_type parameter to kg_query_global for targeted searches", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "afae6563-4491-466b-9d64-80b901b4c4be", "title": "Current Global Meta-Graph Schema", "description": null, "content": "# Current Global Meta-Graph Schema ## Stable Node Types | Node Type | Description | Key Properties | |-----------|-------------|----------------| | Decision | Design/architectural decisions | id, title, rationale, status, confidence | | Constraint | System behavior constraints | id, rule, when, then, source_spec_id | | Alternative | Considered but discarded options | id, title, reason_discarded, parent_decision_id | | Bug | Registered bugs with root cause | id, title, severity, root_cause, status | | Learning | Lessons learned from bugs | id, title, area, confidence | | ORN | Operational Requirement Node | id, title, content, source_artifact_ref | ## Stable Relationship Types | Relationship | From | To | Description | |-------------|------|-----|-------------| | SUPERSEDES | Decision | Decision | One decision replaces another | | CONTRADICTS | Decision | Decision | Two decisions conflict | | VALIDATES | Learning | Bug | Learning derived from bug analysis | | RELATES_TO | Any | Any | General relationship between nodes | | SUPERTYPE_OF | Decision | Constraint | Decision creates a constraint | | HAS_DECISION | Spec | Decision | Spec owns decisions | ## Vector Indexes - Decision HNSW index on title embeddings for semantic search - Hybrid ranking: 0.5*semantic + 0.2*graph_centrality + 0.2*recency + 0.1*confidence", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "c78d6e4e-76eb-4491-83d5-af5d9e57313e", "title": "Consolidation Pipeline Architecture", "description": null, "content": "# Consolidation Pipeline Architecture ## Flow 1. **begin_consolidation**: Opens transactional session, computes SHA256(board + artifact + content) for dedup 2. **add_node_candidate**: Adds node candidates to in-memory session (unique candidate_id per session) 3. **add_edge_candidate**: Adds edge candidates referencing in-session or existing KG nodes (kg: prefix) 4. **get_similar_nodes**: Deterministic fallback uses title-prefix match; production uses HNSW k-NN 5. **propose_reconciliation**: Computes ADD/UPDATE/SUPERSEDE/NOOP hints based on SHA256 comparison 6. **commit_consolidation**: Atomic commit — Kùzu writes + audit row + outbox event ## Session Properties - TTL: 1 hour (configurable via kg_session_ttl_seconds) - Exclusive to authenticated agent - Aborted sessions leave no partial writes in Kùzu ## Outbox Pattern Events written to outbox table during commit, consumed by async workers. No external message broker needed. Workers run consolidation → KG write → event dispatch pipeline.", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 6, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 3, "cards_done": 0}}

This allows validation of each addition before proceeding.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "63b2d919-619c-47e0-9cfb-8bd77c36870c", "question": "Do we need to update the global meta-graph schema or can new node types be added dynamically?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Schema update needed. Kùzu requires explicit node type definitions. Each new node type needs CREATE NODE TABLE statements and corresponding edge types for relationships.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "64eba288-3e1a-4657-b725-c841c4f01413", "question": "When adding Bug nodes to global discovery, should we include the full bug details (severity, steps_to_reproduce) or just metadata (title, linked_card_id, status)? Full details provide more context but increase global graph size.", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Include full bug details. The global discovery layer is meant to provide actionable context — severity and linked_card_id are essential for cross-board pattern recognition. The graph size increase is acceptable given the value of having complete bug context available during discovery queries.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "334f0732-e0b3-4054-bebb-e748b5691ec5", "question": "Should the consolidation pipeline for new node types use the same SHA256 dedup pattern as existing types, or do Bug/Learning nodes need different dedup strategies since they're more dynamic?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Use the same SHA256 dedup pattern. Bug and Learning nodes are identified by their source artifact ID (card_id or bug_id), so content-based dedup works the same way. The deterministic_candidates extraction just needs to include the new node type identifiers.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "e695d8aa-9753-41b5-8ea3-49a0b5e2a3e4", "question": "How should we handle ACL enforcement for cross-board discovery of Bug nodes? Should bugs be visible across all boards the agent has access to, or restricted by some additional criteria?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Bugs visible across all boards the agent has access to. This follows the existing ACL model — kg_query_global already filters by board access. No additional restrictions needed beyond the standard board-level ACL.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "699b68e0-8091-4774-9a3e-a9807ec168a2", "question": "Should we test the HNSW index with real embeddings in e2e tests or rely entirely on stub mode?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Both. Stub mode for unit/integration tests (fast, deterministic). Real embeddings in a dedicated e2e test suite marked with @pytest.mark.e2e that can be skipped during CI. The e2e tests verify that the hybrid ranking formula produces correct ordering with real vectors.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "5fa3faf8-7c38-4796-a3d2-d69712f4e8f4", "question": "Do we need to test the cross-board global discovery ACL filtering in unit tests?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Yes. ACL filtering is security-critical. Test that agents only see results from boards they have access to. Mock the board access list and verify that kg_query_global respects it. This should be a dedicated test file: test_kg_acl_filtering.py.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "679f77f4-9259-426c-92f2-38ea8e84542c", "question": "Which KG node type should be tested first?", "question_type": "choice", "choices": [{"id": "opt_0", "label": "Decision (most complex)"}, {"id": "opt_1", "label": "Constraint (derived from decisions)"}, {"id": "opt_2", "label": "Bug (external integration)"}], "answer": null, "selected": ["opt_0"], "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "knowledge_bases": [{"id": "bfc8aa02-3b95-49ae-a568-ea9271566256", "title": "Current Global Meta-Graph Schema", "description": "[propagated from parent] Current state of the global meta-graph with existing and missing node types", "content": "# Current Global Meta-Graph Schema ## Existing Node Types (5) - **Decision**: title, rationale, context, alternatives_considered, status, confidence - **Entity**: name, description, type, source_artifact_ref - **Criterion**: title, given, when, then, scenario_type, status - **Constraint**: title, rule, when, then, confidence - **Requirement**: title, description, type (functional/technical), index ## Existing Edge Types - supersedes (Decision→Decision) - contradicts (Decision→Decision) - relates_to (generic) - tests (Criterion→Requirement) - validates (Learning→Bug) ## Missing Node Types (6) - **Bug**: title, severity, expected_behavior, observed_behavior, steps_to_reproduce, linked_card_id - **Learning**: title, content, area, source_bug_id - **SpecNode**: title, description, status, board_id - **SprintNode**: title, objective, expected_outcome, status, spec_ref - **CardNode**: title, status, priority, card_type, spec_ref - **Alternative**: title, decision_ref, reason_discarded, alternatives_considered ## Schema Update Required Each new node type needs: CREATE NODE TABLE statement in global meta-graph initialization", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "0eaebbdc-a40a-484f-a699-846eaca8a317", "title": "Consolidation Pipeline Extension Pattern", "description": "[propagated from parent] Pattern for extending the consolidation pipeline to new node types", "content": "# Consolidation Pipeline Extension Pattern ## How Existing Types Are Consolidated 1. `begin_consolidation(board_id, artifact_type, artifact_id, raw_content)` — opens session 2. Extract deterministic_candidates from raw content (node type, title, fields) 3. `add_node_candidate(session_id, candidate)` — adds to in-memory session 4. `add_edge_candidate(session_id, candidate)` — adds relationships 5. `get_similar_nodes(session_id, candidate_id)` — checks for existing similar nodes 6. `propose_reconciliation(session_id)` — computes ADD/UPDATE/SUPERSEDE/NOOP hints 7. `commit_consolidation(session_id)` — atomic write to Kùzu ## Extension Steps for New Node Types 1. Add node type extraction logic in deterministic_candidates builder 2. Define CREATE NODE TABLE for global meta-graph schema 3. Map board-specific fields to global node properties 4. Define edge types for cross-node relationships 5. Update kg_query_global to include new types in search scope 6. Verify ACL enforcement filters correctly for new types ## Key Code Paths - okto_pulse.core.kg.consolidation: pipeline orchestration - okto_pulse.core.kg.global_meta_graph: schema and query layer - okto_pulse.core.kg.discovery: cross-board search", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "a5d1e7c4-a38f-4a52-b074-d2c8469f8293", "title": "Global Discovery Query Layer", "description": "[propagated from parent] How global discovery queries work and what needs updating for new node types", "content": "# Global Discovery Query Layer ## Current Implementation - `kg_query_global(board_id, nl_query, top_k)` — cross-board semantic search - Returns matching decisions from all boards agent has access to - ACL filtering applied automatically based on board membership ## Query Flow 1. Embed the natural language query (sentence-transformers or stub) 2. Search global meta-graph using HNSW index (or title-prefix match fallback) 3. Filter results by board ACL 4. Return top_k results with similarity scores ## Extension Requirements - New node types must be included in the search scope - Each node type may need custom embedding strategy (e.g., Bug nodes embed severity + description) - Discovery queries should support filtering by node type - Consider adding node_type parameter to kg_query_global for targeted searches", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "afae6563-4491-466b-9d64-80b901b4c4be", "title": "Current Global Meta-Graph Schema", "description": null, "content": "# Current Global Meta-Graph Schema ## Stable Node Types | Node Type | Description | Key Properties | |-----------|-------------|----------------| | Decision | Design/architectural decisions | id, title, rationale, status, confidence | | Constraint | System behavior constraints | id, rule, when, then, source_spec_id | | Alternative | Considered but discarded options | id, title, reason_discarded, parent_decision_id | | Bug | Registered bugs with root cause | id, title, severity, root_cause, status | | Learning | Lessons learned from bugs | id, title, area, confidence | | ORN | Operational Requirement Node | id, title, content, source_artifact_ref | ## Stable Relationship Types | Relationship | From | To | Description | |-------------|------|-----|-------------| | SUPERSEDES | Decision | Decision | One decision replaces another | | CONTRADICTS | Decision | Decision | Two decisions conflict | | VALIDATES | Learning | Bug | Learning derived from bug analysis | | RELATES_TO | Any | Any | General relationship between nodes | | SUPERTYPE_OF | Decision | Constraint | Decision creates a constraint | | HAS_DECISION | Spec | Decision | Spec owns decisions | ## Vector Indexes - Decision HNSW index on title embeddings for semantic search - Hybrid ranking: 0.5*semantic + 0.2*graph_centrality + 0.2*recency + 0.1*confidence", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "c78d6e4e-76eb-4491-83d5-af5d9e57313e", "title": "Consolidation Pipeline Architecture", "description": null, "content": "# Consolidation Pipeline Architecture ## Flow 1. **begin_consolidation**: Opens transactional session, computes SHA256(board + artifact + content) for dedup 2. **add_node_candidate**: Adds node candidates to in-memory session (unique candidate_id per session) 3. **add_edge_candidate**: Adds edge candidates referencing in-session or existing KG nodes (kg: prefix) 4. **get_similar_nodes**: Deterministic fallback uses title-prefix match; production uses HNSW k-NN 5. **propose_reconciliation**: Computes ADD/UPDATE/SUPERSEDE/NOOP hints based on SHA256 comparison 6. **commit_consolidation**: Atomic commit — Kùzu writes + audit row + outbox event ## Session Properties - TTL: 1 hour (configurable via kg_session_ttl_seconds) - Exclusive to authenticated agent - Aborted sessions leave no partial writes in Kùzu ## Outbox Pattern Events written to outbox table during commit, consumed by async workers. No external message broker needed. Workers run consolidation → KG write → event dispatch pipeline.", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 6, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 3, "cards_done": 0}}

This test suite verifies governance gates, status transitions, and data integrity across all stages of the workflow. Key test scenarios: 1. **Golden Path**: Full lifecycle from ideation creation through task completion with conclusion recording 2. **Bug Workflow**: Bug card creation linked to origin task, severity tracking, resolution verification 3. **Decision Supersession**: Decision chain A→B→C within spec context, coverage gate enforcement 4. **Sprint Gates**: Sprint creation → task assignment → test coverage gate → evaluation → closure Test structure uses async test fixtures with temp database (from conftest), marked with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective execution in CI. Tests run on main branch merges only, not on every PR.", "context": "## In Scope - golden_path_full_lifecycle - bug_workflow_coverage - decision_supersession_in_spec - sprint_gates_and_evaluations - status_transition_verification - conclusion_recording - governance_gate_integration ## Out of Scope - performance_benchmarks - load_testing - concurrent_user_simulation - CI_pipeline_configuration ## Analysis No end-to-end test currently covers the complete user journey. Individual tests pass but integration gaps between stages may only appear in full workflows. The E2E suite should include the golden path plus 3 key variations (bug workflow, decision supersession, sprint gates) for a total of 4 test scenarios. This balances coverage with runtime constraints. Uses existing conftest fixtures (temp SQLite, session-scoped event loop) and follows the @pytest.mark.e2e convention already established in the codebase. ## Decisions - Use async test fixtures with temp database from conftest - Limit to 4 test scenarios max (golden path + 3 variations) - Mark all tests with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective CI execution - Run on main branch merges only, not every Must be evaluated (scope assessment with complexity score) - Status transitions: draft → review → approved → evaluating → done ## Refinement → Done - Must have in_scope and out_of_scope defined - Status transitions: draft → review → approved → done ## Spec → Validated - Coverage gates: AC, FR, TR, Contract must be covered - Validation gate: completeness, assertiveness, ambiguity scores with thresholds - Status: draft → review → approved → validated (content lock) ## Tasks → Done - Must have conclusion, completeness score, drift score with justifications - Validation: confidence, estimated_completeness, estimated_drift scores - Routing: success → done, failed → not_started ## Sprint → Closed - Gates: draft→active requires cards, active→review requires test coverage, review→closed requires evaluation - Test coverage gate: scoped scenarios must have linked test cards", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "48335cf7-182a-4a52-8d94-7d5935f6519b", "title": "E2E Test Infrastructure Setup", "description": "[propagated from parent] Test infrastructure setup from conftest for E2E tests", "content": "# E2E Test Infrastructure ## Conftest Configuration ```python # Session-scoped event loop (auto mode) DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///:memory:" # temp SQLite KG_EMBEDDING_MODE = "stub" # no real embeddings KG_CLEANUP_ENABLED = "false" # prevent cleanup during tests ``` ## Fixtures - `_db_init` (session): Initializes database schema once per session - `_reset_singletons` (function): Clears global state per test - Temp SQLite database with full transaction support ## Test Markers - `@pytest.mark.e2e`: Marks E2E tests for selective execution - CI default: `pytest -m 'not e2e'` (skip E2E) - Full suite: `pytest` (includes E2E on main merges) ## Async Pattern - All service methods are async - Tests use pytest-asyncio auto mode - No explicit @pytest.mark.asyncio needed", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "db9b4ea0-9454-4267-9037-7eea365adba6", "title": "Bug Workflow Test Scenario", "description": "[propagated from parent] Bug workflow test scenario details for E2E coverage", "content": "# Bug Workflow Test Scenario ## Flow 1. Create a task card (origin_task_id) 2. Create bug card linked to origin_task_id with severity, expected/observed behavior 3. Verify spec auto-resolved from origin task 4. Move bug through lifecycle: not_started → in_progress → validation → done 5. Verify conclusion recorded with root cause ## Key Assertions - Bug card requires origin_task_id, severity, expected_behavior, observed_behavior - Spec auto-resolved correctly from origin task - Severity field enforced (critical/major/minor) - Bug conclusions include root cause analysis", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "23ee61ea-86da-436c-83e4-63116b7dca7f", "title": "Pipeline Governance Gates Reference", "description": null, "content": "# Pipeline Governance Gates Reference ## Spec Validation Gate Runs AFTER deterministic coverage gates (AC/FR/TR/Contract). Three dimensions: - **Completeness** (0-100): How complete is the spec detail? - **Assertiveness** (0-100): How measurable/testable is the text? - **Ambiguity** (0-100, LOWER IS BETTER): How many sentences admit multiple interpretations? Outcome: FAILED if any threshold violated OR recommendation=reject. SUCCESS promotes spec from 'approved' to 'validated'. ## Sprint Gates - **draft→active**: Requires at least one linked card - **active→review**: Requires scoped test coverage (all scenarios have tasks) - **review→closed**: Requires evaluation submission with approval ## Task Validation Gate Three dimensions: confidence, estimated_completeness, estimated_drift. Thresholds resolved from sprint→spec→board hierarchy. Auto-routes: success→done, failed→not_started. ## Deterministic Coverage Gates (pre-validation) - AC coverage: All acceptance criteria must have linked test scenarios - FR coverage: All functional requirements must be covered by BRs/contracts/scenarios - TR coverage: All technical requirements must have linked tasks - Contract coverage: All API contracts must have linked tasks", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "facf8950-f63e-4b14-9d51-87728ea6deab", "title": "Test Database Fixture Pattern", "description": null, "content": "# Test Database Fixture Pattern ## conftest.py Setup ```python import pytest import asyncio # Auto mode with session-scoped event loop pytest_plugins = ["pytest_asyncio"] @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def event_loop(): loop = asyncio.new_event_loop() yield loop loop.close() @pytest.fixture(scope="session", autouse=True) async def _db_init(): """Initialize temp SQLite database for all tests.""" from okto_pulse.core.database import init_db os.environ["DATABASE_URL"] = "sqlite:///file::memory:" await init_db() yield # Cleanup handled by session teardown @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) async def _reset_singletons(): """Clear singleton state between tests.""" yield clear_all_singletons() ``` ## Environment Variables Set by conftest - `DATABASE_URL`: Temp SQLite in-memory database - `KG_BASE_DIR`: Temporary directory for KG files - `KG_EMBEDDING_MODE`: "stub" — no real embedding computation - `KG_CLEANUP_ENABLED`: "false" — prevents background cleanup during tests ## Resource Leak Detection Run with: `python -W error::ResourceWarning -m pytest` Critical test files: test_kg_schema_lifecycle.py, test_kg_file_handles.py", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 6, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 3, "cards_done": 0}}

Duration column helps identify slow tests for optimization.", "author_id": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "order": 0}], "qa_items": [{"id": "6e0eea89-973b-48ef-b604-0f802b45b06a", "question": "Should the E2E suite test the golden path only, or include variations like bug workflows and decision supersession?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Include key variations. Golden path is essential but bug workflow and decision supersession are common enough to warrant coverage. Keep it focused — 3-4 test scenarios max to avoid excessive runtime.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "33473c1c-5469-4ada-a7dd-65a44b8bc659", "question": "Should E2E tests be run in CI on every PR or only on main branch merges?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Only on main branch merges. E2E tests are slower and should be gated. Use the existing @pytest.mark.e2e marker — CI runs 'not e2e' by default and full suite on merge.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "6dc7fc9e-aae6-403e-8a7b-cf60b4df72fd", "question": "Should the golden path E2E test include KG consolidation verification, or focus purely on governance gates and status transitions? Including KG adds complexity but verifies the full pipeline.", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Focus on governance gates and status transitions. KG consolidation is tested separately in dedicated KG tests. The E2E suite should verify the workflow pipeline — if KG integration breaks, the KG-specific tests will catch it. Keep E2E focused on the user journey.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "0d2f848f-ff62-4164-a3c9-f7531ca0afce", "question": "What's the acceptable runtime for the full E2E suite? We need to balance coverage with CI constraints.", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Target under 30 seconds for the full suite. With 4 test scenarios and temp SQLite, this should be achievable. If any single test exceeds 10 seconds, investigate bottlenecks. The session-scoped event loop and shared DB initialization help keep overhead low.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "85e9b3d6-5dea-43c2-a23b-c51e6f15e534", "question": "Should sprint gate tests verify test coverage requirements (scoped scenarios must have linked test cards) or just the status transition mechanics?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Verify both. Test coverage requirements are part of the sprint gate — scoped scenarios must have linked test cards before moving to review. Test that the gate blocks promotion when coverage is incomplete and allows it when satisfied.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "953186c3-ddcc-4289-bca5-d2c4772f5519", "question": "Should pipeline e2e tests verify the in-process outbox event delivery or just check final state?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Both. Verify outbox events are written during the test (intermediate state), and also verify final state after worker consumption. The outbox pattern means events may be delayed — tests should wait for event delivery with a timeout rather than assuming immediate processing.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "c451d92e-a6fa-4a4f-ab8c-ba35f1116ff1", "question": "Should we test the analytics service with real data or mocked aggregates?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Real data. Create a known set of entities (ideations, specs, cards in various states) and verify analytics returns correct counts and percentages. Mocked aggregates only test the response format, not the computation logic. Real data tests catch aggregation bugs.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "8d5c1aa9-cc79-4b7d-906c-0eceb9c9a5a9", "question": "Which analytics metric type should be tested first?", "question_type": "choice", "choices": [{"id": "opt_0", "label": "Overview (basic counts)"}, {"id": "opt_1", "label": "Funnel (conversion rates)"}, {"id": "opt_2", "label": "Coverage (quality metrics)"}], "answer": null, "selected": ["opt_0"], "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "knowledge_bases": [{"id": "69179223-86d6-4cfb-bd7b-ff198ebdb7b1", "title": "Pipeline Stage Governance Gates", "description": "[propagated from parent] Governance gates at each pipeline stage that E2E tests must verify", "content": "# Pipeline Stage Governance Gates ## Ideation → Done - Must be evaluated (scope assessment with complexity score) - Status transitions: draft → review → approved → evaluating → done ## Refinement → Done - Must have in_scope and out_of_scope defined - Status transitions: draft → review → approved → done ## Spec → Validated - Coverage gates: AC, FR, TR, Contract must be covered - Validation gate: completeness, assertiveness, ambiguity scores with thresholds - Status: draft → review → approved → validated (content lock) ## Tasks → Done - Must have conclusion, completeness score, drift score with justifications - Validation: confidence, estimated_completeness, estimated_drift scores - Routing: success → done, failed → not_started ## Sprint → Closed - Gates: draft→active requires cards, active→review requires test coverage, review→closed requires evaluation - Test coverage gate: scoped scenarios must have linked test cards", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "48335cf7-182a-4a52-8d94-7d5935f6519b", "title": "E2E Test Infrastructure Setup", "description": "[propagated from parent] Test infrastructure setup from conftest for E2E tests", "content": "# E2E Test Infrastructure ## Conftest Configuration ```python # Session-scoped event loop (auto mode) DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///:memory:" # temp SQLite KG_EMBEDDING_MODE = "stub" # no real embeddings KG_CLEANUP_ENABLED = "false" # prevent cleanup during tests ``` ## Fixtures - `_db_init` (session): Initializes database schema once per session - `_reset_singletons` (function): Clears global state per test - Temp SQLite database with full transaction support ## Test Markers - `@pytest.mark.e2e`: Marks E2E tests for selective execution - CI default: `pytest -m 'not e2e'` (skip E2E) - Full suite: `pytest` (includes E2E on main merges) ## Async Pattern - All service methods are async - Tests use pytest-asyncio auto mode - No explicit @pytest.mark.asyncio needed", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "db9b4ea0-9454-4267-9037-7eea365adba6", "title": "Bug Workflow Test Scenario", "description": "[propagated from parent] Bug workflow test scenario details for E2E coverage", "content": "# Bug Workflow Test Scenario ## Flow 1. Create a task card (origin_task_id) 2. Create bug card linked to origin_task_id with severity, expected/observed behavior 3. Verify spec auto-resolved from origin task 4. Move bug through lifecycle: not_started → in_progress → validation → done 5. Verify conclusion recorded with root cause ## Key Assertions - Bug card requires origin_task_id, severity, expected_behavior, observed_behavior - Spec auto-resolved correctly from origin task - Severity field enforced (critical/major/minor) - Bug conclusions include root cause analysis", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "23ee61ea-86da-436c-83e4-63116b7dca7f", "title": "Pipeline Governance Gates Reference", "description": null, "content": "# Pipeline Governance Gates Reference ## Spec Validation Gate Runs AFTER deterministic coverage gates (AC/FR/TR/Contract). Three dimensions: - **Completeness** (0-100): How complete is the spec detail? - **Assertiveness** (0-100): How measurable/testable is the text? - **Ambiguity** (0-100, LOWER IS BETTER): How many sentences admit multiple interpretations? Outcome: FAILED if any threshold violated OR recommendation=reject. SUCCESS promotes spec from 'approved' to 'validated'. ## Sprint Gates - **draft→active**: Requires at least one linked card - **active→review**: Requires scoped test coverage (all scenarios have tasks) - **review→closed**: Requires evaluation submission with approval ## Task Validation Gate Three dimensions: confidence, estimated_completeness, estimated_drift. Thresholds resolved from sprint→spec→board hierarchy. Auto-routes: success→done, failed→not_started. ## Deterministic Coverage Gates (pre-validation) - AC coverage: All acceptance criteria must have linked test scenarios - FR coverage: All functional requirements must be covered by BRs/contracts/scenarios - TR coverage: All technical requirements must have linked tasks - Contract coverage: All API contracts must have linked tasks", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "facf8950-f63e-4b14-9d51-87728ea6deab", "title": "Test Database Fixture Pattern", "description": null, "content": "# Test Database Fixture Pattern ## conftest.py Setup ```python import pytest import asyncio # Auto mode with session-scoped event loop pytest_plugins = ["pytest_asyncio"] @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def event_loop(): loop = asyncio.new_event_loop() yield loop loop.close() @pytest.fixture(scope="session", autouse=True) async def _db_init(): """Initialize temp SQLite database for all tests.""" from okto_pulse.core.database import init_db os.environ["DATABASE_URL"] = "sqlite:///file::memory:" await init_db() yield # Cleanup handled by session teardown @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) async def _reset_singletons(): """Clear singleton state between tests.""" yield clear_all_singletons() ``` ## Environment Variables Set by conftest - `DATABASE_URL`: Temp SQLite in-memory database - `KG_BASE_DIR`: Temporary directory for KG files - `KG_EMBEDDING_MODE`: "stub" — no real embedding computation - `KG_CLEANUP_ENABLED`: "false" — prevents background cleanup during tests ## Resource Leak Detection Run with: `python -W error::ResourceWarning -m pytest` Critical test files: test_kg_schema_lifecycle.py, test_kg_file_handles.py", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 6, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 3, "cards_done": 0}}

Key test areas: - **BoardService**: Board CRUD, member management, guideline linking - **AgentService**: Agent registration, permissions, preset assignment - **GuidelineService**: Guideline CRUD, board linking, tag filtering - **CommentService**: Comment CRUD, choice comments, response tracking - **QAService**: Question/answer lifecycle, choice questions, deletion - **SpecService**: Spec CRUD, evaluation submission, validation gates - **CardService**: Card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions, conclusions - **IdeationService**: Ideation lifecycle, evaluations, complexity assessment - **RefinementService**: Refinement lifecycle, spec derivation, scope management - **SprintService**: Sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations, task assignment Uses shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest. Tests real service-to-service dependencies while mocking external dependencies (KG, event bus).", "context": "## In Scope - all_18_service_classes_CRUD - error_path_testing - edge_case_coverage - service_to_service_dependencies - shared_test_database_fixture - per_test_reset_strategy ## Out of Scope - API_route_testing - MCP_tool_testing - external_dependency_integration - performance_benchmarks ## Analysis The service layer is the core business logic with 18 service classes, most lacking integration tests. Service-to-service dependencies must be tested with real interactions (not mocked) while external dependencies (KG, event bus) are mocked. The existing conftest provides temp SQLite DB with session-scoped event loop and per-test reset capability. Each service gets a dedicated test class covering create/read/update/delete plus edge cases specific to that service's domain. ## Decisions - Use shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest - Test real service-to-service dependencies - Mock only external dependencies (KG, event bus) - Dedicated test class per service - Cover CRUD plus service-specific edge cases", "status": The outbox worker consumption path is tested separately in event bus tests. This keeps service tests focused and fast.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "d8b2ac0c-1f18-4587-9291-f1c0c3bc77b9", "question": "For services that interact with the KG (SpecService, CardService), should we use KG_EMBEDDING_MODE=stub for all tests, or include at least one test with real embeddings?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Use KG_EMBEDDING_MODE=stub for all service integration tests. These are common failure modes — duplicate board names, invalid status transitions, foreign key violations on deleted entities. BoardService — board CRUD, member management 2. AgentService — agent registration, permissions 3. GuidelineService — guideline CRUD, board linking 4. CommentService — comment CRUD, choice comments, responses 5. QAService — question/answer lifecycle 6. SpecService — spec CRUD, evaluation, validation gates 7. CardService — card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions 8. IdeationService — ideation lifecycle, evaluations 9. RefinementService — refinement lifecycle, spec derivation 10. SprintService — sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations ... and 8 more covering KG, analytics, presets, etc. ## Dependencies - All services interact with database (SQLAlchemy async) - Some services interact with event bus (in-process outbox) - Some services interact with KG (consolidation pipeline) - Service-to-service dependencies exist (e.g., CardService → SpecService for spec_id validation) ## Testing Strategy - Test real service-to-service dependencies - Each test gets clean database state via _reset_singletons - Temp SQLite provides real transaction behavior - No cross-test contamination ## Service Instantiation - Services use singleton pattern in production - Tests must reset singletons to get fresh instances - Database connections reused across session (efficient)", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "e0c0c875-c448-4a7a-8291-4fa1d28edde7", "title": "Service-Specific Edge Cases", "description": "[propagated from parent] Key edge cases per service that integration tests should cover", "content": "# Service-Specific Edge Cases ## BoardService - Duplicate board names (if enforced) - Board with no agents vs full member list - Guideline linking/unlinking cascade ## CardService - Circular dependency detection (auto-blocked) - Status transition validation (state machine) - Moving to done without conclusion (blocked) - Conclusion required fields validation ## SpecService - Validation gate threshold violations - Content lock after validation success - Coverage gate enforcement (AC/FR/TR/Contract) - Superseded spec handling ## IdeationService - Status transition validation (state machine) - Complexity computation from scope scores - Snapshot creation on done status ## SprintService - Gate checks: cards required for active, coverage for review, evaluation for closed - Test scenario scoping to sprints", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "897c9f8c-7cb1-4855-9e4f-024c9c484a9c", "title": "Service Layer Architecture Overview", "description": null, "content": "# Service Layer Architecture Overview ## 18 Service Classes ### Core Services 1. **IdeationService** — CRUD for ideations, evaluation, derivation to specs 2. **RefinementService** — CRUD for refinements, scope management, spec derivation 3. **SpecService** — CRUD for specs, validation gates, card derivation 4. **CardService** — CRUD for cards, status transitions, dependencies 5. **SprintService** — Sprint lifecycle, gate enforcement, evaluation ### KG Services 6. **KGConsolidationService** — Consolidation pipeline (begin/add/commit/abort) 7. **KGQueryService** — Natural language search, Cypher queries, global discovery 8. **KGSemanticService** — Similarity search, contradiction detection, supersedence chains ### Governance Services 9. **ValidationService** — Spec validation gate, task validation, threshold resolution 10. **CoverageService** — Deterministic coverage gates (AC/FR/TR/Contract) 11. **AnalyticsService** — Overview, funnel, quality, velocity, coverage metrics ### Communication Services 12. **QAService** — Q&A management across all entity types 13. **CommentService** — Comments, choice boards, responses 14. **NotificationService** — @mentions, unseen tracking ### Infrastructure Services 15. **AuthService** — Provider pattern ABC, concrete implementations 16. **StorageService** — Provider pattern ABC, file attachments 17. **OutboxService** — In-process event bus, worker coordination 18. **GuidelineService** — Board guidelines, catalog management ## Provider Pattern Auth and storage are ABCs in core. Concrete implementations live in consuming package (community edition). Core has no hardcoded providers.", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "0ea26c8f-b623-4476-98dd-bc49fce6a259", "title": "Test Infrastructure Setup", "description": null, "content": "# Test Infrastructure Setup ## pytest-asyncio Configuration ```python # pyproject.toml or conftest.py [tool.pytest.ini_options] asyncio_mode = "auto" ``` Session-scoped event loop: ```python @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def event_loop(): loop = asyncio.new_event_loop() yield loop loop.close() ``` ## Required Environment Variables | Variable | Value | Purpose | |----------|-------|---------| | DATABASE_URL | sqlite:///file::memory: | Temp in-memory database | | KG_BASE_DIR | /tmp/test_kg_{uuid} | Isolated KG directory per test run | | KG_EMBEDDING_MODE | stub | No real embedding computation | | KG_CLEANUP_ENABLED | false | Prevent background cleanup during tests | ## Singleton Reset Pattern ```python @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) async def _reset_singletons(): """Clear singleton state between tests to prevent cross-test contamination.""" yield from okto_pulse.core import clear_all_singletons clear_all_singletons() ``` ## Resource Leak Detection ```bash python -W error::ResourceWarning -m pytest tests/test_kg_schema_lifecycle.py tests/test_kg_file_handles.py ```", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 6, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 3, "cards_done": 0}}

Duration column helps identify slow services for optimization.", "author_id": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "order": 0}], "qa_items": [{"id": "a8e32485-2c11-4c20-a6bd-13c2d9dd4dff", "question": "Should service integration tests use the real database or an in-memory SQLite? Real DB gives better coverage but slower execution.", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Use the existing conftest temp SQLite DB. It's already configured for session-scoped event loop and provides real transaction behavior without the overhead of a full PostgreSQL instance.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "e392ff47-a6a7-442b-87cc-8ab898d976dc", "question": "Should we test service-to-service dependencies (e.g., BoardService calling AgentService) or mock all cross-service calls?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Test real service-to-service dependencies for integration tests. Mock only external dependencies (KG, event bus). The point of integration tests is to verify services work together correctly.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "60180df1-811e-40d4-888c-c315685e5ff8", "question": "Should we test event bus integration (in-process outbox) as part of service integration tests, or mock the event bus and test it separately? The event bus writes to an outbox table — testing this requires understanding the worker consumption path.", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Mock the event bus for service integration tests. The outbox worker consumption path is tested separately in event bus tests. Service tests should verify that events are published to the outbox (check the outbox table), but not that workers consume them correctly. This keeps service tests focused and fast.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "d8b2ac0c-1f18-4587-9291-f1c0c3bc77b9", "question": "For services that interact with the KG (SpecService, CardService), should we use KG_EMBEDDING_MODE=stub for all tests, or include at least one test with real embeddings?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Use KG_EMBEDDING_MODE=stub for all service integration tests. Real embeddings add external dependency and slow down tests. The stub mode is already configured in conftest and provides sufficient coverage — KG-specific embedding quality is tested in dedicated KG tests.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "cf8d6b42-b617-4203-9deb-5e8c74071116", "question": "Should service integration tests cover error paths like database constraint violations, duplicate key errors, and foreign key violations, or focus on happy path with edge cases?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Cover error paths including database constraint violations. These are common failure modes — duplicate board names, invalid status transitions, foreign key violations on deleted entities. Each service should have at least 2-3 error path tests covering the most likely failures in that domain.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "3c3fdab5-d26c-47d6-a85e-d348c96c625e", "question": "Should we test the outbox service with real async workers or mock the worker consumption?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Mock the worker consumption for unit tests (fast, deterministic). Use real workers in a dedicated e2e test that verifies the full outbox→worker→consumption pipeline. The mock tests verify event format and ordering, while e2e tests verify timing and delivery guarantees.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "7210f9c6-872b-4382-8ca8-d169e364d86d", "question": "Should parse_multi_value tests cover edge cases like empty strings, single values, and mixed separators?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Yes. Test cases: empty string returns [], single value "foo" returns ["foo"], pipe-separated "a|b|c" returns ["a","b","c"], comma-separated handled per context, whitespace trimming on each value, and mixed separators behavior documented. This is used by MCP tools for parameter parsing so edge cases matter.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "645a8357-74cb-4d3d-84bd-a18e001742a3", "question": "Which service class should be tested first?", "question_type": "choice", "choices": [{"id": "opt_0", "label": "SpecService (core)"}, {"id": "opt_1", "label": "KGConsolidationService (complex)"}, {"id": "opt_2", "label": "AnalyticsService (canonical)"}], "answer": null, "selected": ["opt_0"], "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "knowledge_bases": [{"id": "508cf431-ac55-42ee-8134-b7498b811688", "title": "Service Layer Architecture Overview", "description": "[propagated from parent] Overview of all 18 service classes and their interaction patterns", "content": "# Service Layer Architecture ## 18 Service Classes 1. BoardService — board CRUD, member management 2. AgentService — agent registration, permissions 3. GuidelineService — guideline CRUD, board linking 4. CommentService — comment CRUD, choice comments, responses 5. QAService — question/answer lifecycle 6. SpecService — spec CRUD, evaluation, validation gates 7. CardService — card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions 8. IdeationService — ideation lifecycle, evaluations 9. RefinementService — refinement lifecycle, spec derivation 10. SprintService — sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations ... and 8 more covering KG, analytics, presets, etc. ## Dependencies - All services interact with database (SQLAlchemy async) - Some services interact with event bus (in-process outbox) - Some services interact with KG (consolidation pipeline) - Service-to-service dependencies exist (e.g., CardService → SpecService for spec_id validation) ## Testing Strategy - Test real service-to-service dependencies - Mock external: KG, event bus worker consumption - Use shared test database fixture with per-test reset", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "81a19a4c-bf50-4136-8e84-42b820139bee", "title": "Test Database Fixture Pattern", "description": "[propagated from parent] How the test database fixture works with per-test reset strategy", "content": "# Test Database Fixture Pattern ## Conftest Setup ```python @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def _db_init(): """Initialize temp SQLite database once per session""" DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///:memory:" # Apply inline migrations from database.py yield @pytest.fixture def _reset_singletons(): """Clear global state per test""" # Reset service singletons, clear caches yield # Cleanup after test ``` ## Per-Test Isolation - Each test gets clean database state via _reset_singletons - Temp SQLite provides real transaction behavior - No cross-test contamination ## Service Instantiation - Services use singleton pattern in production - Tests must reset singletons to get fresh instances - Database connections reused across session (efficient)", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "e0c0c875-c448-4a7a-8291-4fa1d28edde7", "title": "Service-Specific Edge Cases", "description": "[propagated from parent] Key edge cases per service that integration tests should cover", "content": "# Service-Specific Edge Cases ## BoardService - Duplicate board names (if enforced) - Board with no agents vs full member list - Guideline linking/unlinking cascade ## CardService - Circular dependency detection (auto-blocked) - Status transition validation (state machine) - Moving to done without conclusion (blocked) - Conclusion required fields validation ## SpecService - Validation gate threshold violations - Content lock after validation success - Coverage gate enforcement (AC/FR/TR/Contract) - Superseded spec handling ## IdeationService - Status transition validation (state machine) - Complexity computation from scope scores - Snapshot creation on done status ## SprintService - Gate checks: cards required for active, coverage for review, evaluation for closed - Test scenario scoping to sprints", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "897c9f8c-7cb1-4855-9e4f-024c9c484a9c", "title": "Service Layer Architecture Overview", "description": null, "content": "# Service Layer Architecture Overview ## 18 Service Classes ### Core Services 1. **IdeationService** — CRUD for ideations, evaluation, derivation to specs 2. **RefinementService** — CRUD for refinements, scope management, spec derivation 3. **SpecService** — CRUD for specs, validation gates, card derivation 4. **CardService** — CRUD for cards, status transitions, dependencies 5. **SprintService** — Sprint lifecycle, gate enforcement, evaluation ### KG Services 6. **KGConsolidationService** — Consolidation pipeline (begin/add/commit/abort) 7. **KGQueryService** — Natural language search, Cypher queries, global discovery 8. **KGSemanticService** — Similarity search, contradiction detection, supersedence chains ### Governance Services 9. **ValidationService** — Spec validation gate, task validation, threshold resolution 10. **CoverageService** — Deterministic coverage gates (AC/FR/TR/Contract) 11. **AnalyticsService** — Overview, funnel, quality, velocity, coverage metrics ### Communication Services 12. **QAService** — Q&A management across all entity types 13. **CommentService** — Comments, choice boards, responses 14. **NotificationService** — @mentions, unseen tracking ### Infrastructure Services 15. **AuthService** — Provider pattern ABC, concrete implementations 16. **StorageService** — Provider pattern ABC, file attachments 17. **OutboxService** — In-process event bus, worker coordination 18. **GuidelineService** — Board guidelines, catalog management ## Provider Pattern Auth and storage are ABCs in core. Concrete implementations live in consuming package (community edition). Core has no hardcoded providers.", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "0ea26c8f-b623-4476-98dd-bc49fce6a259", "title": "Test Infrastructure Setup", "description": null, "content": "# Test Infrastructure Setup ## pytest-asyncio Configuration ```python # pyproject.toml or conftest.py [tool.pytest.ini_options] asyncio_mode = "auto" ``` Session-scoped event loop: ```python @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def event_loop(): loop = asyncio.new_event_loop() yield loop loop.close() ``` ## Required Environment Variables | Variable | Value | Purpose | |----------|-------|---------| | DATABASE_URL | sqlite:///file::memory: | Temp in-memory database | | KG_BASE_DIR | /tmp/test_kg_{uuid} | Isolated KG directory per test run | | KG_EMBEDDING_MODE | stub | No real embedding computation | | KG_CLEANUP_ENABLED | false | Prevent background cleanup during tests | ## Singleton Reset Pattern ```python @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) async def _reset_singletons(): """Clear singleton state between tests to prevent cross-test contamination.""" yield from okto_pulse.core import clear_all_singletons clear_all_singletons() ``` ## Resource Leak Detection ```bash python -W error::ResourceWarning -m pytest tests/test_kg_schema_lifecycle.py tests/test_kg_file_handles.py ```", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 6, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 3, "cards_done": 0}}

{"id": "fea52138-d18d-4079-b3ed-7204712e2470", "board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "title": "Agent Permission Enforcement Test Suite", "description": "Security-critical test suite covering the full permission system including preset CRUD, agent-level overrides, and granular flag enforcement at the MCP tool layer. This refinement ensures that permission controls are correctly enforced and that unauthorized operations are properly blocked. Key test areas: - **Preset CRUD**: Create/read/update/delete permission presets (full_access, read_only, custom) - **Agent Override**: Apply override to agent, verify tool access changes immediately - **Granular Flags**: Toggle individual boolean flags, verify enforcement at MCP layer - **Inheritance**: Agent inherits from preset, override specific tools, verify precedence - **Default Permissions**: New agents receive correct default permissions - **MCP Tool Enforcement**: Denied tools return proper error responses (403/permission_denied) - **Admin Bypass**: Admin agents bypass all restrictions; non-admin cannot bypass", "context": "## In Scope - preset_CRUD - agent_permission_override - granular_flag_enforcement - MCP_tool_access_control - admin_bypass - default_permissions - inheritance_chain ## Out of Scope - board_share_permissions - enterprise_auth_integration - role_based_access_control - external_identity_providers ## Analysis The permission system controls which agents can access which MCP tools — a security-critical feature currently without test coverage. Gaps here could allow unauthorized operations. Tests must cover both the permission service layer (logic verification) and MCP tool enforcement layer (runtime access control). Admin bypass is a critical path that must be tested positively (admins CAN access all) and negatively (non-admins CANNOT bypass). ## Decisions - Test against actual MCP tool invocation path - Cover both service layer and MCP enforcement layer - Include positive and negative admin bypass tests - Verify preset inheritance with override precedence - Test default permissions on agent creation", Tests verify default preset assignment, override application, and admin flag handling."}], "decisions": [{"id": "dec_97e89864", "title": "Use mock MCP middleware for permission enforcement tests", "rationale": "Testing permission enforcement at the actual MCP layer requires full server startup with event loops, database connections, and middleware chains. By mocking the MCP tool middleware, we can isolate the permission resolution logic and test it deterministically without infrastructure dependencies. This approach gives faster feedback, easier debugging, and more focused assertions on permission behavior.", "context": "Permission enforcement tests for MCP tools", "alternatives_considered": ["Full integration test with real MCP server", "Unit test at service layer only", "End-to-end test with HTTP client"], "supersedes_decision_id": null, "linked_requirements": ["2", "5", "6"], "linked_task_ids": null, "status": "active", "notes": "Mock middleware intercepts tool_name + agent_id and returns allow/deny. Tests verify the resolution chain: preset → override → effective permissions."}, {"id": "dec_f2298c6c", "title": "Fail-closed default for unregistered tools", "rationale": "A fail-closed design ensures that new MCP tools added to the system are not automatically accessible by existing agents. This prevents accidental exposure of powerful or dangerous tools. Agents must explicitly receive permission grants for each tool, and the middleware denies access by default when a tool is not found in the agent's effective permissions registry.", "context": "MCP tool permission middleware default behavior", "alternatives_considered": ["Fail-open (allow unregistered tools)", "Admin-only default for unregistered tools", "Preset-level opt-in for new tools"], "supersedes_decision_id": null, "linked_requirements": ["5", "6"], "linked_task_ids": null, "status": "active", "notes": "This decision directly supports the fail-closed business rule. Tests must verify that unknown tools are denied even for non-admin agents with broad presets."}], "decisions_stats": {"total": 2, "active": 2, "superseded": 0, "revoked": 0, "other": 0}, "evaluations": [], "skip_test_coverage": false, "skip_rules_coverage": false, "skip_decisions_coverage": false, "skip_qualitative_validation": false, "validation_threshold": null, "cards": [], "sprints": [], "screen_mockups": [{"id": "sm_8b767c72", "title": "Agent Permission Configuration Panel", "description": "UI panel for configuring agent permissions — preset selection, override toggles, and admin flag", "screen_type": "panel", "html_content": "

This is the key behavior tested by scenario ts_515e4ef8.", "author_id": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "order": 0}], "qa_items": [{"id": "6780399d-ee7f-4238-a86c-120e33189041", "question": "Should permission tests include MCP tool-level enforcement verification, or just the service layer?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Both. Service layer tests verify permission logic, MCP enforcement tests verify that denied tools actually return errors at the API boundary. Both layers are security-critical.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "ff5dc549-6315-4b12-a39c-bd04785a60f8", "question": "Do we need to test the admin bypass path where admin agents can access all tools regardless of permissions?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Yes, admin bypass is a critical path. Test that admin agents can call any tool regardless of permission settings. Also test that non-admin agents cannot bypass restrictions.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "a54f9c8c-6532-4544-9c8e-6f54b58f64f0", "question": "Should permission override tests verify that changes take effect immediately without requiring agent restart, or is there a caching layer that delays propagation?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Changes should take effect immediately. The permission system reads from the database on each tool invocation — there's no caching layer. Test that applying an override and immediately calling a tool reflects the new permissions.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "bf33ac12-a236-44a8-bd15-91c834b7f451", "question": "How should we test the MCP tool enforcement layer — do we invoke actual MCP tools with permission-denied agents, or mock the tool invocation and verify the permission check middleware?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Invoke actual MCP tools with permission-denied agents for integration tests. This verifies the full enforcement path from permission check through tool rejection. Mock only for unit-level service tests focusing on permission logic in isolation.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "19e990f1-0c6f-4eaa-9b6a-5541cff0a8d6", "question": "Should we test permission inheritance chains (preset → agent override → granular flag) as a single integrated test or as separate isolated tests?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Both. Isolated tests for each level (preset, override, granular) ensure individual components work correctly. An integrated chain test verifies that precedence is correct: granular flag > agent override > preset default.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "f0412aaa-9e7a-414b-866c-a07bc05f0790", "question": "Should the test suite verify that permission changes take effect immediately without requiring agent restart or session refresh?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "Yes — permission changes must take effect immediately on the next MCP tool invocation. The middleware resolves effective permissions on every request, so no caching or session refresh is required. Tests should verify this by: (1) creating an agent with a preset, (2) modifying the preset or adding overrides, and (3) confirming the next tool call reflects the change without restart.", "selected": null, "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}, {"id": "5d84c2f4-df18-4437-b6e3-10d0c2f3d50e", "question": "What is the expected behavior when an agent's permission preset is deleted while agents still reference it? Should tests cover this orphan scenario?", "question_type": "text", "choices": null, "answer": "When a preset is deleted, agents referencing it should fall back to the default (read-only) preset. Tests should include an orphan scenario: create agent with custom preset → delete preset → verify agent falls back to default permissions. Agents start with minimal access and permissions are explicitly granted as needed, following the principle of least privilege.", "selected": ["opt_0"], "asked_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "answered_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a"}], "knowledge_bases": [{"id": "9e616134-0e85-4e00-b09b-cea7ae8b5ca4", "title": "Permission System Architecture", "description": "[propagated from parent] Architecture of the permission system including presets, overrides, and enforcement", "content": "# Permission System Architecture ## Components - **Presets**: Named permission sets (full_access, read_only, custom) with boolean flags per tool category - **Agent Overrides**: Per-agent customization that overrides preset defaults for specific tools - **Granular Flags**: Individual boolean permissions on Agent model controlling MCP access - **Admin Bypass**: Admin agents bypass all permission restrictions ## Precedence (highest to lowest) 1. Admin status — bypasses everything 2. Granular flag override — explicit per-tool setting 3. Agent preset override — agent-level customization 4. Preset default — inherited from assigned preset ## MCP Enforcement - Each MCP tool invocation checks agent permissions before execution - Denied tools return error response with permission_denied detail - Permission check happens in middleware layer before tool handler ## Testing Implications - Must test all precedence levels independently and together - Admin bypass needs both positive (admin CAN) and negative (non-admin CANNOT) tests - MCP enforcement must be tested at the actual invocation boundary", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "d087cd1b-77ee-4a75-9880-23a420e96a62", "title": "MCP Tool Permission Middleware", "description": "[propagated from parent] How MCP tools enforce permissions at runtime", "content": "# MCP Tool Permission Middleware ## Flow 1. Client sends tool request with agent API key 2. Middleware resolves agent from API key 3. Checks if agent is admin → bypass all checks 4. Resolves effective permissions: preset → override → granular flag 5. If permission denied → return error, do not invoke tool handler 6. If permission granted → proceed to tool handler ## Error Response - Tool invocation blocked with permission_denied error - Error includes tool name and required permission for debugging - No partial execution — all-or-nothing per tool ## Test Strategy - Create agents with known permission states - Invoke tools and verify correct allow/deny behavior - Test edge cases: missing preset, conflicting overrides, empty permissions", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "b531a3a0-a176-4bd3-9d3c-a747c915e85f", "title": "Agent Model Permission Fields", "description": "[propagated from parent] Agent model fields related to permissions and how they interact", "content": "# Agent Model Permission Fields ## Schema ```python class Agent(Base): # Permission-related fields preset_id: Optional[str] # Linked permission preset permissions: Dict[str, bool] # Granular flag overrides is_admin: bool # Admin bypass flag ``` ## Default Permissions - New agents receive preset-based defaults - No preset → restricted defaults (minimal access) - Admin agents created with is_admin=True bypass all checks ## Override Behavior - permissions dict merges with preset defaults - Explicit True/False in override takes precedence over preset - Missing key in override → falls back to preset value", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "34e66867-0dad-4dc5-8f0d-b7c59d9488ab", "title": "Agent Model Permission Fields Reference", "description": "Reference for Agent model permission fields, override structure, and resolution algorithm", "content": "# Agent Model Permission Fields ## Overview The Agent model stores permission configuration at the instance level. Each agent has a reference to a permission preset and optional overrides. ## Schema Fields | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | `permission_preset_id` | UUID \| NULL | Reference to the PermissionPreset this agent inherits from | | `permission_overrides` | JSONB | Per-tool boolean overrides that take precedence over preset | | `is_admin` | Boolean | When true, bypasses all permission restrictions | | `effective_permissions` | Computed | Resolved permissions after applying preset + overrides | ## Permission Overrides Format ```json { "tool_name": { "allowed": true, "source": "override" // "preset" or "override" } } ``` ## Resolution Algorithm 1. If `is_admin` → all tools allowed 2. Load preset by `permission_preset_id` 3. Apply `permission_overrides` (overrides take precedence) 4. Result is `effective_permissions` ## Default Behavior - New agents without explicit preset receive the "default" preset - Default preset grants read-only MCP tool access - Admin flag defaults to false on creation", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "81a5fec5-3e5b-4524-b0ac-36421f7b9130", "title": "MCP Tool Permission Middleware Reference", "description": "Reference for MCP tool permission middleware flow, enforcement logic, and error response format", "content": "# MCP Tool Permission Middleware ## Overview The permission middleware intercepts every MCP tool invocation and enforces access control before the tool handler executes. ## Middleware Flow ``` Client Request → MCP Router → Permission Middleware → Tool Handler ↓ Check Agent Permissions ↓ Allowed? → Pass Through / Denied? → 403 Error ``` ## Enforcement Logic 1. Extract agent identity from connection context (API key / session) 2. Resolve effective permissions (preset + overrides, admin bypass check) 3. Look up tool name against allowed tools list 4. If allowed → pass request to handler 5. If denied → return structured error response ## Error Response Format ```json { "error": { "code": "permission_denied", "message": "Agent does not have permission to use tool: ", "tool_name": "", "agent_id": "" } } ``` ## HTTP Status Codes - `403 Forbidden` — Permission denied for non-admin agents - `200 OK` — Tool executed successfully (permissions passed) - `401 Unauthorized` — Agent identity could not be resolved ## Bypass Conditions - `is_admin = true` → always allowed, middleware returns immediately - Tool not in permission registry → defaults to denied (fail-closed)", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 2, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 0, "cards_done": 0}}

Result is `effective_permissions` ## Default Behavior - New agents without explicit preset receive the "default" preset - Default preset grants read-only MCP tool access - Admin flag defaults to false on creation", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}, {"id": "81a5fec5-3e5b-4524-b0ac-36421f7b9130", "title": "MCP Tool Permission Middleware Reference", "description": "Reference for MCP tool permission middleware flow, enforcement logic, and error response format", "content": "# MCP Tool Permission Middleware ## Overview The permission middleware intercepts every MCP tool invocation and enforces access control before the tool handler executes. ## Middleware Flow ``` Client Request → MCP Router → Permission Middleware → Tool Handler ↓ Check Agent Permissions ↓ Allowed? → Pass Through / Denied? → 403 Error ``` ## Enforcement Logic 1. Extract agent identity from connection context (API key / session) 2. Resolve effective permissions (preset + overrides, admin bypass check) 3. Look up tool name against allowed tools list 4. If allowed → pass request to handler 5. If denied → return structured error response ## Error Response Format ```json { "error": { "code": "permission_denied", "message": "Agent does not have permission to use tool: ", "tool_name": "", "agent_id": "" } } ``` ## HTTP Status Codes - `403 Forbidden` — Permission denied for non-admin agents - `200 OK` — Tool executed successfully (permissions passed) - `401 Unauthorized` — Agent identity could not be resolved ## Bypass Conditions - `is_admin = true` → always allowed, middleware returns immediately - Tool not in permission registry → defaults to denied (fail-closed)", "mime_type": "text/markdown"}], "coverage_summary": {"acceptance_criteria_total": 7, "acceptance_criteria_covered": 0, "uncovered_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], "test_scenarios_total": 3, "business_rules_total": 2, "api_contracts_total": 1, "cards_total": 0, "cards_done": 0}}

{"id": "460ee29c-7880-4693-b84f-202c54fbec4c", "board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "title": "Decision CRUD Lifecycle Test Suite", "description": "Comprehensive test suite covering the full lifecycle of decision management operations. This refinement focuses on testing add_decision, update_decision, remove_decision, supersede chain tracking, and linked_requirements/task associations. The tests verify that decisions correctly transition through active → superseded → revoked states and that the supersession chain is maintained accurately.

Key test areas:
- **Add Decision**: All fields including title, rationale, context, alternatives_considered, linked_requirements, notes
- **Update Decision**: Partial updates, full updates, CLEAR operations on optional fields
- **Remove Decision**: Soft-delete to revoked status, history preservation
- **Supersede Chain**: A → B → C chain with automatic status management and active tracking
- **Linked Requirements**: Pipe-separated FR index resolution and validation
- **Coverage Gate**: Opt-in coverage gate enforcement for decisions", "context": "## In Scope
- add_decision
- update_decision
- remove_decision
- supersede_decision
- linked_requirements
- coverage_gate_enforcement
- decision_status_transitions

## Out of Scope
- KG_consolidation_pipeline
- global_discovery
- semantic_search_quality
- decision_vector_embedding

## Analysis
Decision management is a critical feature driving the Knowledge Graph consolidation pipeline. Currently no test file covers this lifecycle, meaning regressions could silently corrupt the KG. The test suite should use pytest fixtures for decision setup and mock the KG service for service-level tests, with at least one integration test verifying end-to-end KG node creation. Supersession chains must be verified fully (A→B→C), not just adjacent pairs.

## Decisions
- Use pytest fixtures for decision setup
- Mock KG service for service-level tests
- Include one integration test for end-to-end KG node creation
- Verify full supersession chains (A→B→C)
- Test both active and revoked decision states", "functional_requirements": ["Test add_decision endpoint creates a decision with title, rationale and optional fields", "Test update_decision modifies existing decision fields correctly", "Test remove_decision soft-deletes decision by setting status to revoked", "Test supersede flow auto-moves target decision to superseded status", "Test decision linked_task_ids are populated when tasks link to decisions", "Test decision coverage gate validates active decisions have at least one linked task", "Test decisions_stats summary is computed correctly in spec context"], "technical_requirements": [{"id": "tr_31f2afcb", "text": "Tests must use pytest-asyncio with session-scoped event loop", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_303443c6", "text": "Use conftest _db_init fixture for database initialization", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_c1270ca3", "text": "KG_EMBEDDING_MODE=stub required for decision embedding tests", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_0a4f545f", "text": "Each test must be isolated - no shared state between tests", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_8db09178", "text": "Test fixtures must clean up created decisions after each test", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_d693f301", "text": "Must mock KG consolidation worker to avoid async background tasks interfering", "linked_task_ids": []}], "acceptance_criteria": ["All 7 functional requirement tests pass with 100% coverage", "No ResourceWarning or unhandled exceptions in test output", "Decision CRUD operations complete under 500ms each", "Supersede chain trace returns correct depth and current_active node", "Coverage gate correctly blocks promotion when active decisions lack linked tasks", "Test cleanup leaves no orphaned decisions in database"], "status": "approved", "version": 2, "assignee_id": null, "created_by": "eca54b56-d4cf-44c3-904a-f07bf778799a", "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:41", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:48:18", "labels": ["testing", "decisions", "lifecycle"], "cards": [{"id": "532af542-91f5-4931-9bb1-864a3dbbf5c3", "title": "Implement add_decision and update_decision tests", "status": "done", "priority": "high", "assignee_id": null}, {"id": "07766f8b-b8f0-457c-9a1f-d0b339e52c86", "title": "Implement remove_decision and supersede chain tests", "status": "in_progress", "priority": "high", "assignee_id": null}, {"id": "a008f019-8e01-4bcf-9d9d-e985ef0a9b36", "title": "Implement linked_requirements resolution tests", "status": "started", "priority": "medium", "assignee_id": null}, {"id": "4e550c86-3643-4b3a-8596-22fb2fde7157", "title": "Implement coverage gate enforcement tests", "status": "not_started", "priority": "medium", "assignee_id": null}]}
