{"id": "fea52138-d18d-4079-b3ed-7204712e2470", "board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "title": "Agent Permission Enforcement Test Suite", "description": "Security-critical test suite covering the full permission system including preset CRUD, agent-level overrides, and granular flag enforcement at the MCP tool layer. This refinement ensures that permission controls are correctly enforced and that unauthorized operations are properly blocked.

Key test areas:
- **Preset CRUD**: Create/read/update/delete permission presets (full_access, read_only, custom)
- **Agent Override**: Apply override to agent, verify tool access changes immediately
- **Granular Flags**: Toggle individual boolean flags, verify enforcement at MCP layer
- **Inheritance**: Agent inherits from preset, override specific tools, verify precedence
- **Default Permissions**: New agents receive correct default permissions
- **MCP Tool Enforcement**: Denied tools return proper error responses (403/permission_denied)
- **Admin Bypass**: Admin agents bypass all restrictions; non-admin cannot bypass", "context": "## In Scope
- preset_CRUD
- agent_permission_override
- granular_flag_enforcement
- MCP_tool_access_control
- admin_bypass
- default_permissions
- inheritance_chain

## Out of Scope
- board_share_permissions
- enterprise_auth_integration
- role_based_access_control
- external_identity_providers

## Analysis
The permission system controls which agents can access which MCP tools — a security-critical feature currently without test coverage. Gaps here could allow unauthorized operations. Tests must cover both the permission service layer (logic verification) and MCP tool enforcement layer (runtime access control). Admin bypass is a critical path that must be tested positively (admins CAN access all) and negatively (non-admins CANNOT bypass).

## Decisions
- Test against actual MCP tool invocation path
- Cover both service layer and MCP enforcement layer
- Include positive and negative admin bypass tests
- Verify preset inheritance with override precedence
- Test default permissions on agent creation", "functional_requirements": ["Test permission preset CRUD (create/read/update/delete)", "Test agent-level permission override application", "Test granular boolean flag toggling and enforcement at MCP layer", "Test permission inheritance from preset with specific tool overrides", "Test default permissions assigned to new agents", "Test MCP tool enforcement — denied tools return proper errors", "Test admin bypass — admin agents access all tools regardless of permissions"], "technical_requirements": [{"id": "tr_c9a0bc6a", "text": "Tests must verify both service layer permission logic and MCP tool enforcement boundary", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_16dc8387", "text": "Use pytest-asyncio with session-scoped event loop", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_82de7074", "text": "Mock MCP tool middleware to test enforcement without full server startup", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_d3e88c64", "text": "Permission flags on Agent model: per-tool boolean access control", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_10f1b34b", "text": "Admin agents have is_admin flag that bypasses all restrictions", "linked_task_ids": []}, {"id": "tr_44a8b962", "text": "Preset definitions are stored as named permission sets", "linked_task_ids": []}], "acceptance_criteria": ["All 7 functional requirement tests pass with full coverage", "MCP tool enforcement returns correct error codes for denied tools", "Admin bypass verified for admin and non-admin agents", "Permission inheritance chain: preset → agent override → effective permissions", "Default permissions correct for new agent creation", "No permission escalation possible through edge cases", "Test cleanup leaves no orphaned permission records"], "status": "approved", "permissions"], "cards": [{"id": "fe921199-33c1-479c-93df-1b67d7d8a4e9", "title": "Implement preset CRUD and default permission tests", "status": "not_started", "priority": "high", "assignee_id": null}, {"id": "596fbed0-69a4-43ef-86b9-4ce6f2396031", "title": "Implement agent override and granular flag enforcement tests", "status": "done", "priority": "high", "assignee_id": null}, {"id": "d6dba2bb-ddd8-4584-b579-301a5590f12f", "title": "Implement admin bypass and inheritance tests", "status": "done", "priority": "high", "assignee_id": null}]}

{"id": "cc08b776-cd18-455e-b9c1-2777509aab24", "board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "title": "Global Meta-Graph Node Type Expansion", "description": "Extend the global discovery layer from 5 to all 11 node types, enabling cross-board discovery of Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode, and Alternative nodes. This refinement implements the consolidation pipeline extensions required for each new node type.

Key implementation areas:
- **Bug Nodes**: Bug reports linked to cards/tasks, discoverable across boards with severity metadata
- **Learning Nodes**: Lessons learned from bugs and decisions, connected via validates relationship
- **SpecNode**: Spec-level knowledge for cross-board reference and dependency tracking
- **SprintNode**: Sprint context across boards for velocity and planning insights
- **CardNode**: Task cards discoverable globally for cross-project task correlation
- **Alternative Nodes**: Considered alternatives from decisions, including reason_discarded

Each node type requires: CREATE NODE TABLE in global meta-graph schema, consolidation pipeline mapping, edge type definitions for relationships, ACL enforcement across board boundaries, and discovery query updates.", "context": "## In Scope
- Bug_node_type
- Learning_node_type
- SpecNode_type
- SprintNode_type
- CardNode_type
- Alternative_node_type
- consolidation_pipeline_extension
- global_schema_update
- ACL_enforcement_cross_board

## Out of Scope
- HNSW_vector_index_migration
- semantic_search_quality_tuning
- embedding_model_updates
- vector_similarity_threshold_tuning

## Analysis
Global discovery is currently incomplete — only 5 of 11 node types are discoverable across boards. Bug and Learning nodes provide the highest user value as they enable cross-project pattern recognition and lessons learned sharing. Implementation should be incremental: Bug/Learning first (highest value), then SpecNode/CardNode (workflow), then SprintNode/Alternative. Each addition requires explicit Kùzu schema updates (CREATE NODE TABLE) and consolidation pipeline mappings. ACL enforcement must prevent unauthorized cross-board access.

## Decisions
- Use existing consolidation pipeline pattern for new node types
- Implement incrementally by priority (Bug/Learning first)
- Require explicit Kùzu schema CREATE NODE TABLE for each type
- Enforce ACL across board boundaries
- Update global discovery queries to include all new types", "functional_requirements": ["Test all stable node types in KG schema (Decision, Constraint, Alternative, Bug, Learning, ORN)", "Test relationship types between nodes (SUPERSEDES, CONTRADICTS, VALIDATES, RELATES_TO)", "Test HNSW vector index search with hybrid ranking", "Test supersede chain traversal up to depth 10", "Test contradiction detection between decision pairs", "Test semantic similarity search with min_similarity threshold", "Test cross-board global discovery layer with ACL filtering"], {"id": "cc08b776-cd18-455e-b9c1-2777509aab24", "board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "title": "Global Meta-Graph Node Type Expansion", "description": "Extend the global discovery layer from 5 to all 11 node types, enabling cross-board discovery of Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode, and Alternative nodes. This refinement implements the consolidation pipeline extensions required for each new node type.

Key implementation areas:
- **Bug Nodes**: Bug reports linked to cards/tasks, discoverable across boards with severity metadata
- **Learning Nodes**: Lessons learned from bugs and decisions, connected via validates relationship
- **SpecNode**: Spec-level knowledge for cross-board reference and dependency tracking
- **SprintNode**: Sprint context across boards for velocity and planning insights
- **CardNode**: Task cards discoverable globally for cross-project task correlation
- **Alternative Nodes**: Considered alternatives from decisions, including reason_discarded

Each node type requires: CREATE NODE TABLE in global meta-graph schema, consolidation pipeline mapping, edge type definitions for relationships, ACL enforcement across board boundaries, and discovery query updates.", "context": "## In Scope
- Bug_node_type
- Learning_node_type
- SpecNode_type
- SprintNode_type
- CardNode_type
- Alternative_node_type
- consolidation_pipeline_extension
- global_schema_update
- ACL_enforcement_cross_board

## Out of Scope
- HNSW_vector_index_migration
- semantic_search_quality_tuning
- embedding_model_updates
- vector_similarity_threshold_tuning

## Analysis
Global discovery is currently incomplete — only 5 of 11 node types are discoverable across boards. Bug and Learning nodes provide the highest user value as they enable cross-project pattern recognition and lessons learned sharing. Implementation should be incremental: Bug/Learning first (highest value), then SpecNode/CardNode (workflow), then SprintNode/Alternative. Each addition requires explicit Kùzu schema updates (CREATE NODE TABLE) and consolidation pipeline mappings. ACL enforcement must prevent unauthorized cross-board access.

## Decisions
- Use existing consolidation pipeline pattern for new node types
- Implement incrementally by priority (Bug/Learning first)
- Require explicit Kùzu schema CREATE NODE TABLE for each type
- Enforce ACL across board boundaries
- Update global discovery queries to include all new types", "functional_requirements": ["Test all stable node types in KG schema (Decision, Constraint, Alternative, Bug, Learning, ORN)", "Test This refinement defines the test structure, fixture strategy, and coverage targets for service-level integration testing.

Key test areas:
- **BoardService**: Board CRUD, member management, guideline linking
- **AgentService**: Agent registration, permissions, preset assignment
- **GuidelineService**: Guideline CRUD, board linking, tag filtering
- **CommentService**: Comment CRUD, choice comments, response tracking
- **QAService**: Question/answer lifecycle, choice questions, deletion
- **SpecService**: Spec CRUD, evaluation submission, validation gates
- **CardService**: Card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions, conclusions
- **IdeationService**: Ideation lifecycle, evaluations, complexity assessment
- **RefinementService**: Refinement lifecycle, spec derivation, scope management
- **SprintService**: Sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations, task assignment

Uses shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest. Tests real service-to-service dependencies while mocking external dependencies (KG, event bus).", "context": "## In Scope

- error_path_testing
- edge_case_coverage
- service_to_service_dependencies
- shared_test_database_fixture
- per_test_reset_strategy

## Out of Scope
- API_route_testing
- MCP_tool_testing
- external_dependency_integration
- performance_benchmarks

## Analysis
The service layer is the core business logic with 18 service classes, most lacking integration tests. Service-to-service dependencies must be tested with real interactions (not mocked) while external dependencies (KG, event bus) are mocked. The existing conftest provides temp SQLite DB with session-scoped event loop and per-test reset capability. Each service gets a dedicated test class covering create/read/update/delete plus edge cases specific to that service's domain.

## Decisions
- Use shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest
- Test real service-to-service dependencies
- Mock only external dependencies (KG, event bus)
- Dedicated test class per service
- Cover CRUD plus service-specific edge cases", "functional_requirements": ["Test all 18 service classes with integration test coverage", "Test provider pattern (auth/storage ABCs) with concrete {"id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "title": "Full Pipeline E2E Test Orchestration", "description": "Umbrella E2E validation suite exercising the complete pipeline: ideation → refinement → spec → tasks → sprint → done. This test suite verifies governance gates, status transitions, and data integrity across all stages of the workflow.

Key test scenarios:
1. **Golden Path**: Full lifecycle from ideation creation through task completion with conclusion recording
2. **Bug Workflow**: Bug card creation linked to origin task, severity tracking, resolution verification
3. **Decision Supersession**: Decision chain A→B→C within spec context, coverage gate enforcement
4. **Sprint Gates**: Sprint creation → task assignment → test coverage gate → evaluation → closure

Test structure uses async test fixtures with temp database (from conftest), marked with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective execution in CI. Tests run on main branch merges only, not on every PR.", "context": "## In Scope
- golden_path_full_lifecycle
- bug_workflow_coverage
- decision_supersession_in_spec
- sprint_gates_and_evaluations
- status_transition_verification
- conclusion_recording
- governance_gate_integration

## Out of Scope
- performance_benchmarks
- load_testing
- concurrent_user_simulation
- CI_pipeline_configuration

## Analysis
No end-to-end test currently covers the complete user journey. Individual tests pass but integration gaps between stages may only appear in full workflows. The E2E suite should include the golden path plus 3 key variations (bug workflow, decision supersession, sprint gates) for a total of 4 test scenarios. This balances coverage with runtime constraints. Uses existing conftest fixtures (temp SQLite, session-scoped event loop) and follows the @pytest.mark.e2e convention already established in the codebase.

## Decisions
- Use async test fixtures with temp database from conftest
- Limit to 4 test scenarios max (golden path + 3 variations)
- Mark all tests with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective CI execution
- Run on main branch merges only, not every PR
- Verify governance gates at each pipeline stage", "functional_requirements": ["Test full ideation-to-card pipeline from creation through completion", "Test spec validation gate with coverage checks (AC, FR, TR, Contract)", "Test sprint lifecycle (draft→active→review→closed) with gates", "Test card dependency management and circular dependency detection", "Test test scenario lifecycle and task linkage", "Test evaluation submission for specs and sprints", "Test analytics service aggregation across all pipeline stages"], "technical_requirements": [{"id": "tr_25ee932d", "text": "E2E tests use @pytest.mark.e2e marker and can be skipped with -m 'not e2e'", "linked_task_ids": ["98d46ea2-f283-4a3f-bfa4-f59d30468bba"]}, {"id": "tr_c0873c3d", "text": "Tests must initialize full database schema via conftest _db_init fixture", "linked_task_ids": ["98d46ea2-f283-4a3f-bfa4-f59d30468bba"]}, {"id": "tr_9e7e66de", "text": "Pipeline tests must verify in-process outbox event delivery", ["98d46ea2-f283-4a3f-bfa4-f59d30468bba"]}, {"id": "tr_b6b59a63", "text": "CardType enum identity enforced — use == CardType.NORMAL not string comparison", "linked_task_ids": ["3c096905-0520-4942-b3b9-875de8b2df4d"]}, {"id": "tr_2776d983", "text": "parse_multi_value used for parameter parsing in MCP tools", "linked_task_ids": ["3c096905-0520-4942-b3b9-875de8b2df4d"]}], "acceptance_criteria": ["Full ideation→refinement→spec→card pipeline tested end-to-end", "Spec validation gate correctly enforces coverage thresholds", "Sprint gates (cards required, test coverage, 4. **Sprint Gates**: Sprint creation → task assignment → test coverage gate → evaluation → closure

Test structure uses async test fixtures with temp database (from conftest), marked with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective execution in CI. Tests run on main branch merges only, not on every PR.", "context": "## In Scope
- golden_path_full_lifecycle
- bug_workflow_coverage
- decision_supersession_in_spec
- sprint_gates_and_evaluations
- status_transition_verification
- conclusion_recording
- governance_gate_integration

## Out of Scope
- performance_benchmarks
- load_testing
- concurrent_user_simulation
- CI_pipeline_configuration

## Analysis
No end-to-end test currently covers the complete user journey. Individual tests pass but integration gaps between stages may only appear in full workflows. The E2E suite should include the golden path plus 3 key variations (bug workflow, decision supersession, sprint gates) for a total of 4 test scenarios. This balances coverage with runtime constraints. Uses existing conftest fixtures (temp SQLite, session-scoped event loop) and follows the @pytest.mark.e2e convention already established in the codebase.

## Decisions
- Use async test fixtures with temp database from conftest
- Limit to 4 test scenarios max (golden path + 3 variations)
- Mark all tests with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective CI execution
- Run on main branch merges only, not every PR

No events are lost or duplicated. Event handlers execute to completion.", "linked_requirements": ["Test in-process outbox event bus writes and worker consumption"], "linked_task_ids": ["f2213694-653d-457b-9203-fa9e4ef2ab57"], "notes": null}, {"id": "br_f79d41d8", "title": "Per-board KG isolation with concurrent operations", "rule": "When multiple boards perform concurrent KG operations, then each board's KG instance remains isolated with no cross-contamination.", "when": "Concurrent consolidation or query operations run on different boards simultaneously", "then": "Each board's KG nodes and edges remain isolated. No cross-board data leakage. content.text:
{"board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "total": 5, "offset": 0, "limit": 20, "specs": [{"id": "fea52138-d18d-4079-b3ed-7204712e2470", "title": "Agent Permission Enforcement Test Suite", "description": "Security-critical test suite covering the full permission system including preset CRUD, agent-level overrides, and granular flag enforcement at the MCP tool layer. This refinement ensures that permission controls are correctly enforced and that unauthorized operations are properly blocked.

Key test areas:
- **Preset CRUD**: Create/read/update/delete permission presets (full_access, read_only, custom)
- **Agent Override**: Apply override to agent, verify tool access changes immediately
- **Granular Flags**: Toggle individual boolean flags, verify enforcement at MCP layer
- **Inheritance**: Agent inherits from preset, override specific tools, verify precedence
- **Default Permissions**: New agents receive correct default permissions
- **MCP Tool Enforcement**: Denied tools return proper error responses (403/permission_denied)
- **Admin Bypass**: Admin agents bypass all restrictions; non-admin cannot bypass", "status": "approved", "version": 2, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "security", "permissions"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:07:04", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:48:28"}, {"id": "460ee29c-7880-4693-b84f-202c54fbec4c", "title": "Decision CRUD Lifecycle Test Suite", "description": "Comprehensive test suite covering the full lifecycle of decision management operations. This refinement focuses on testing add_decision, update_decision, remove_decision, supersede chain tracking, and linked_requirements/task associations. The tests verify that decisions correctly transition through active → superseded → revoked states and that the supersession chain is maintained accurately.

Key test areas:
- **Add Decision**: All fields including title, rationale, context, alternatives_considered, linked_requirements, notes
- **Update Decision**: Partial updates, full updates, CLEAR operations on optional fields
- **Remove Decision**: Soft-delete to revoked status, history preservation
- **Supersede Chain**: A → B → C chain with automatic status management and active tracking
- **Linked Requirements**: Pipe-separated FR index resolution and validation
- **Coverage Gate**: Opt-in coverage gate enforcement for decisions", "status": "approved", "version": 2, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "decisions", "lifecycle"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:41", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:48:18"}, {"id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "title": "Full Pipeline E2E Test Orchestration", "description": "Umbrella E2E validation suite exercising the complete pipeline: ideation → refinement → spec → tasks → sprint → done. This test suite verifies governance gates, status transitions, and data integrity across all stages of the workflow.

Key test scenarios:
1. **Golden Path**: Full lifecycle from ideation creation through task completion with conclusion recording
2. **Bug Workflow**: Bug card creation linked to origin task, severity tracking, resolution verification
3. **Decision Supersession**: Decision chain A→B→C within spec context, coverage gate enforcement
4. **Sprint Gates**: Sprint creation → task assignment → test coverage gate → evaluation → closure

Test structure uses async test fixtures with temp database (from conftest), marked with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective execution in CI. Tests run on main branch merges only, not on every PR.", "status": "in_progress", "version": 8, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "e2e", "pipeline"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:45", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:39:48"}, {"id": "cc08b776-cd18-455e-b9c1-2777509aab24", "title": "Global Meta-Graph Node Type Expansion", "description": "Extend the global discovery layer from 5 to all 11 node types, enabling cross-board discovery of Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode, and Alternative nodes. This refinement implements the consolidation pipeline extensions required for each new node type.

Key implementation areas:
- **Bug Nodes**: Bug reports linked to cards/tasks, discoverable across boards with severity metadata
- **Learning Nodes**: Lessons learned from bugs and decisions, connected via validates relationship
- **SpecNode**: Spec-level knowledge for cross-board reference and dependency tracking
- **SprintNode**: Sprint context across boards for velocity and planning insights
- **CardNode**: Task cards discoverable globally for cross-project task correlation
- **Alternative Nodes**: Considered alternatives from decisions, including reason_discarded

Each node type requires: CREATE NODE TABLE in global meta-graph schema, consolidation pipeline mapping, edge type definitions for relationships, ACL enforcement across board boundaries, and discovery query updates.", "status": "in_progress", "version": 8, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["knowledge-graph", "discovery", "architecture"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:43", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:39:45"}, {"id": "477d82be-ec97-4261-a796-c35751af0cf8", "title": "Service Integration Test Framework", "description": "Comprehensive integration test framework covering all 18 service classes with CRUD operations, error paths, and edge cases. This refinement defines the test structure, fixture strategy, and coverage targets for service-level integration testing.

Key test areas:
- **BoardService**: Board CRUD, member management, guideline linking
- **AgentService**: Agent registration, permissions, preset assignment
- **GuidelineService**: Guideline CRUD, board linking, tag filtering
- **CommentService**: Comment CRUD, choice comments, response tracking
- **QAService**: Question/answer lifecycle, choice questions, deletion
- **SpecService**: Spec CRUD, evaluation submission, validation gates
- **CardService**: Card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions, conclusions
- **IdeationService**: Ideation lifecycle, evaluations, complexity assessment
- **RefinementService**: Refinement lifecycle, spec derivation, scope management
- **SprintService**: Sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations, task assignment

Uses shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest. Tests real service-to-service dependencies while mocking external dependencies (KG, event bus).", "status": "in_progress", "version": 8, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "service-layer", "integration"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:48", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:28:12"}]}

content.text: {"total_all": 30, "filtered_count": 7, "offset": 0, "limit": 10, "cards": [{"id": "927363ac-14d0-4936-9c3f-2c4278188475", "title": "Implement golden path E2E test", "description": "Full lifecycle E2E test: create ideation \u2192 move to review \u2192 approve \u2192 create refinement \u2192 derive spec \u2192 create card linked to spec \u2192 complete card workflow. Verify spec auto-resolved from origin task. Move bug through lifecycle: not_started \u2192 in_progress \u2192 validation \u2192 done. Verify conclusion recorded with root cause analysis. Severity field enforced (critical/major/minor).", "status": "done", "priority": "high", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "test_scenario_ids": null, "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "47c63fff-14d7-4f0b-862c-82652b273a52", "title": "Implement sprint gates E2E test", "description": "Sprint creation \u2192 task assignment \u2192 test coverage gate \u2192 evaluation \u2192 closure. Test draft\u2192active requires cards, active\u2192review requires scoped test coverage (all scenarios have linked test cards), review\u2192closed requires evaluation with approval. Verify error messages specify exact gate violations per spec BRs.", "status": "done", "priority": "high", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "test_scenario_ids": null, "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "9d023c9c-3747-43f7-b37c-237eefd90c0f", "title": "Implement decision supersession E2E test", "description": "Decision chain A\u2192B\u2192C within spec context. Create decision A, then B supersedes A (A\u2192superseded), then C supersedes B (B\u2192superseded). Verify coverage gate enforcement: active decisions without linked tasks fail validation gate. Test that linking tasks resolves violations.", "status": "in_progress", "priority": "medium", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "test_scenario_ids": null, "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "98d46ea2-f283-4a3f-bfa4-f59d30468bba", "title": "Test: Full ideation to card pipeline E2E", "description": "E2E test for full ideation\u2192refinement\u2192spec\u2192card pipeline with validation gate enforcement and analytics verification.", "status": "not_started", "priority": "high", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "test_scenario_ids": ["ts_85fdc53a"], "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "e04af3fb-a763-4d91-8b17-c6b0494c7995", "title": "Test: Sprint gate enforcement with missing requirements", "description": "Integration test for sprint gates (cards required, test coverage, evaluation) and card dependency enforcement.", "status": "not_started", "priority": "high", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "test_scenario_ids": ["ts_61d37e5a"], "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "3c096905-0520-4942-b3b9-875de8b2df4d", "title": "Test: Circular card dependency creation blocked", "description": "Unit test for circular dependency detection in card dependencies with bidirectional traceability verification.", "status": "not_started", "priority": "medium", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "test_scenario_ids": ["ts_459c3f7a"], "due_date": null, "labels": []}]}

arguments.content: **Spec-level note (Decision CRUD Lifecycle Test Suite):** This spec covers the complete decision management lifecycle. Priority is on supersede chain testing (A→B→C) as that's the most complex code path in the decision service. The coverage gate enforcement tests are medium priority since they're opt-in. All tests should use real database per the spec decisions — no mocking of the KG service layer.

arguments.content: **Spec-level note (Global Meta-Graph Node Type Expansion):** This spec extends global discovery from 5 to all 11 node types. The consolidation pipeline pattern is well-established for existing types — follow the same CREATE NODE TABLE + edge definition + ACL enforcement pattern. Security-critical: Bug and Learning nodes may contain sensitive data, ensure cross-board ACL filtering works correctly before merging.

arguments.content: **Spec-level note (Full Pipeline E2E Test Orchestration):** This is the umbrella E2E suite covering the entire ideation→refinement→spec→task→sprint pipeline. All tests should be marked @pytest.mark.e2e for selective CI execution. Target runtime under 10 seconds per test using temp database fixtures from conftest. The bug workflow E2E and sprint gates E2E are highest priority as they exercise the most governance logic.

arguments.content: **Spec-level note (Service Integration Test Framework):** Covers all 18 service classes. Use shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest. Tests real service-to-service dependencies while mocking external deps (KG, event bus). BoardService and AgentService tests are foundation — they must pass before other service tests can be reliable. Dependency injection for external mocks per spec decisions.

arguments.content: **Spec-level note (Agent Permission Enforcement Test Suite):** Security-critical spec. All permission tests must verify the MCP tool layer actually blocks denied tools with 403 responses. Admin bypass is a positive test (admin CAN access all) while non-admin bypass is negative (non-admin CANNOT). Fail-closed default for unregistered tools means new tools are denied by default until explicitly granted.

content.text:
{"board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "total": 15, "offset": 0, "limit": 20, "ideations": [{"id": "dd3fc04e-0c2f-4b14-bbdd-f7b57f8214bb", "title": "Implement Service Layer Integration Tests", "description": "Limited integration tests for BoardService, AgentService, GuidelineService, CommentService, QAService.

## Current State
- 18 service classes exist in the core engine
- Most services have unit-level coverage but lack integration tests
- Services interact with database, event bus, and KG — these interactions need verification

## Services Needing Integration Tests
1. BoardService — board CRUD, member management
2. AgentService — agent registration, permissions
3. GuidelineService — guideline CRUD, board linking
4. CommentService — comment CRUD, choice comments, responses
5. QAService — question/answer lifecycle
6. SpecService — spec CRUD, evaluation, validation gates
7. CardService — card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions
8. IdeationService — ideation lifecycle, evaluations
9. RefinementService — refinement lifecycle, spec derivation
10. SprintService — sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations", "problem_statement": "Service layer is the core business logic — needs comprehensive integration coverage. Without integration tests, regressions in service interactions go undetected until production.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "service-layer", "integration"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:33", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:56:04"}, {"id": "ac776dc2-826b-4213-ac6b-81c45d317017", "title": "Split MCP Server into Modular Tool Groups", "description": "The MCP server.py is 10,920 lines in a single file. Split into modular tool groups (boards, cards, specs, kg, etc.) for maintainability.

## Current State
- `server.py` contains all MCP tool definitions in one monolithic file
- Tools cover boards, cards, specs, ideations, refinements, KG operations, sprints, and more
- No separation of concerns between different functional areas

## Benefits of Splitting
- Each module can be tested independently
- New tools can be added without touching the main server file
- Code review becomes manageable (reviewing a 300-line module vs a 10K-line file)
- Import-time performance improves with lazy loading of unused modules", "problem_statement": "Single massive file is unmaintainable and hard to test in isolation. Any change to MCP tools requires loading the entire 10K+ line file. Testing individual tool groups is impossible without mocking everything else.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["architecture", "refactoring", "mcp"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:36:55", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:56:01"}, {"id": "ef370e92-5edf-4a18-abfb-a795764851f6", "title": "Discovery Feature Tests", "description": "Discovery intent catalog, saved searches, and search history are not tested. This is the global cross-board search feature.

## Current State
- Discovery provides cross-board semantic search via global layer
- Intent catalog defines searchable queries/templates
- Saved searches allow users to bookmark frequent searches
- Search history tracks past queries per agent

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Intent Catalog** — CRUD intent templates, execute intents
2. **Saved Searches** — create/read/update/delete saved searches
3. **Search History** — automatic recording, retrieval, pagination
4. **Cross-board ACL** — respect board permissions in search results
5. **Semantic Search** — verify embedding-based matching works
6. **Performance** — search completes within acceptable time bounds
7. **Edge Cases** — empty boards, no results, malformed queries", "problem_statement": "New feature with no automated verification. Discovery is the global cross-board search feature — critical for multi-board workflows but entirely untested.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "draft", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "discovery", "search"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:04", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:45:02"}, {"id": "d2f3f466-c5fb-4010-afed-cb7c7a65623e", "title": "Resolve Version Number Inconsistency", "description": "pyproject.toml says 0.1.5, config.py default is 0.1.4, README says 0.1.3 is current published. Single source of truth needed.

## Current State
- `okto-pulse-core/pyproject.toml`: version = "0.1.5"
- `config.py` default: version = "0.1.4"
- README: references 0.1.3 as current published version
- Three different versions across the codebase

## Impact
- Confusion during deployment and debugging
- Hard to determine actual version from running instance
- Release process doesn't enforce single source of truth", "problem_statement": "Confusion about actual version during deployment and debugging. Different parts of the system report different versions, making troubleshooting difficult.", "complexity": "small", "status": "draft", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["maintenance", "versioning"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:24", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:45:00"}, {"id": "7b4b9c8a-0296-4388-8312-2a52c11d98fd", "title": "Sprint Evaluation/Transition Gate Tests", "description": "Only basic Sprint CRUD is tested. The evaluation and transition gates (draft→active requires cards, active→review requires test coverage, review→closed requires evaluation) need testing.

## Current State
- Sprint state machine: draft → active → review → closed
- Gates enforced at transitions:
  - **draft → active**: requires at least one linked card
  - **active → review**: requires scoped test coverage (all scenarios have test cards)
  - **review → closed**: requires qualitative evaluation submitted
- Basic CRUD operations are tested but gate enforcement is not

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Draft → Active** — success with cards, failure without cards
2. **Active → Review** — success with full coverage, failure with missing tests
3. **Review → Closed** — success with evaluation, failure without
4. **Gate Bypass** — verify skip flags work correctly (skip_test_coverage, etc.)
5. **Evaluation Submission** — submit sprint evaluation, verify scores and thresholds
6. **Invalid Transitions** — draft → closed should fail
7. **Cancelled State** — cancel from any state, verify no further transitions", "problem_statement": "Sprint governance rules unverified by tests. Gate enforcement is critical for workflow integrity — if gates are bypassed, the sprint system loses its value.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "review", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "sprints", "governance"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:32", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:49"}, {"id": "f0e3a3fc-ddc2-4df6-86d3-96e1a03a20c9", "title": "Event Bus Retry/DLQ Flow Tests", "description": "Event bus has retry (exponential backoff, up to 5 attempts) and DLQ semantics but these paths are not tested.

## Current State
- Event bus publishes events to outbox table
- Workers consume with exponential backoff retry (max 5 attempts)
- Failed events after max retries go to Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)
- ConsolidationEnqueuer is the only active handler
- Retry and DLQ logic exists but has never been exercised in tests

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Happy Path** — event published, consumed successfully on first attempt
2. **Retry Success** — handler fails 2 times, succeeds on 3rd attempt
3. **DLQ Transition** — handler fails all 5 attempts, event moves to DLQ
4. **Exponential Backoff** — verify delay between retries follows pattern
5. **DLQ Recovery** — manually reprocess DLQ event
6. **Concurrent Processing** — multiple events processed in parallel
7. **Worker Crash Recovery** — worker dies mid-processing, event retried", "problem_statement": "Failure handling in event pipeline unverified. If retry or DLQ logic has bugs, events could be silently lost or cause infinite retry loops.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "review", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "event-bus", "resilience"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:14", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:46"}, {"id": "09b026de-b16c-484f-a9d2-0084042ae524", "title": "Archive/Restore Tree Cascade Tests", "description": "Archive and restore tree cascade operations have no test file. These are destructive operations that need thorough testing.

## Current State
- `archive_tree` cascades: ideation → refinements → specs → cards
- `restore_tree` reverses the cascade, restoring pre_archive_status
- Both operations set/clear the archived flag recursively
- No test file covers these operations

## Test Scenarios Needed
- Archive ideation → verify all descendants archived
- Archive refinement → verify specs and cards archived
- Archive spec → verify cards archived (not deleted)
- Restore archived tree → verify pre_archive_status restored
- Partial restore → only specified entity restored, others remain archived
- Edge case: archived entity with new children created after archive
- Edge case: concurrent modification during archive", "problem_statement": "Archiving an ideation cascades to refinements, specs, cards — any bug here causes data loss. These are among the most dangerous operations in the system.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "review", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "archive", "safety"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:48", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:44"}, {"id": "7abf1b67-6819-48e3-bdc7-b472fe41a64c", "title": "Add Bug Card Workflow Tests", "description": "No dedicated test file for bug card creation/move/unblock flow. The codebase enforces test-first workflow for bugs but this path isn't tested.

## Current State
- Bug cards have special fields: origin_task_id, severity, expected_behavior, observed_behavior
- Bug cards require origin_task_id and auto-resolve spec from the origin task
- Bug workflow: create → assign fix tasks → link to scenarios → resolve
- No test file covers this entire lifecycle

## Test Scenarios Needed
- Bug creation with all required fields
- Bug creation without required fields (validation errors)
- Moving bug through status transitions
- Linking fix tasks to bugs
- Bug unblocking dependencies
- Severity-based priority enforcement", "problem_statement": "Critical workflow gap in test coverage. Bug cards are a core feature with special constraints, but the entire lifecycle is untested.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "review", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "bugs", "coverage"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:10", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:41"}, {"id": "7d982783-4d2c-4035-854b-ef44b6f9e86b", "title": "Implement ActivityLogHandler and MentionsNotifier for Event Bus", "description": "The event bus has only ConsolidationEnqueuer handler implemented. Phase 2+ features (ActivityLogHandler, MentionsNotificationHandler, MetricsCounterHandler, WebhookDispatchHandler, GlobalLayerSyncHandler) are planned but not built.

## Current State
- Event bus publishes domain events to the outbox
- Only ConsolidationEnqueuer consumes events (for KG consolidation)
- Handler interfaces exist but implementations are stubs or missing

## Handlers Needed
1. **ActivityLogHandler** — records all board activity for audit/history
2. **MentionsNotificationHandler** — detects @mentions and notifies agents
3. **MetricsCounterHandler** — updates analytics counters from events
4. **WebhookDispatchHandler** — forwards events to external webhooks
5. **GlobalLayerSyncHandler** — syncs cross-board global discovery layer", "problem_statement": "Domain events are published but most handlers don't exist yet. ## Current State
- `server.py` contains all MCP tool definitions in one monolithic file
- Tools cover boards, cards, specs, ideations, refinements, KG operations, sprints, and more
- No separation of concerns between different functional areas

## Benefits of Splitting
- Each module can be tested independently
- New tools can be added without touching the main server file
- Code review becomes manageable (reviewing a 300-line module vs a 10K-line file)
- Import-time performance improves with lazy loading of unused modules", "problem_statement": "Single massive file is unmaintainable and hard to test in isolation. ## Current State
- Sprint state machine: draft → active → review → closed
- Gates enforced at transitions:
  - **draft → active**: requires at least one linked card
  - **active → review**: requires scoped test coverage (all scenarios have test cards)
  - **review → closed**: requires qualitative evaluation submitted
- Basic CRUD operations are tested but gate enforcement is not

## Test Scenarios Needed

2. **Active → Review** — success with full coverage, failure with missing tests
3. **Review → Closed** — success with evaluation, failure without
4. **Gate Bypass** — verify skip flags work correctly (skip_test_coverage, etc.)
5. **Evaluation Submission** — submit sprint evaluation, verify scores and thresholds

## Current State
- Event bus publishes events to outbox table
- Workers consume with exponential backoff retry (max 5 attempts)
- Failed events after max retries go to Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)
- ConsolidationEnqueuer is the only active handler
- Retry and DLQ logic exists but has never been exercised in tests

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Happy Path** — event published, consumed successfully on first attempt
2. **Retry Success** — handler fails 2 times, succeeds on 3rd attempt
3. **DLQ Transition** — handler fails all 5 attempts, event moves to DLQ
4. **Exponential Backoff** — verify delay between retries follows pattern
5. **DLQ Recovery** — manually reprocess DLQ event
6. **Concurrent Processing** — multiple events processed in parallel
7. **Worker Crash Recovery** — worker dies mid-processing, event retried", "problem_statement": "Failure handling in event pipeline unverified. content.text:
{"board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "total": 15, "offset": 0, "limit": 200, "ideations": [{"id": "dd3fc04e-0c2f-4b14-bbdd-f7b57f8214bb", "title": "Implement Service Layer Integration Tests", "description": "Limited integration tests for BoardService, AgentService, GuidelineService, CommentService, QAService.

## Current State
- 18 service classes exist in the core engine
- Most services have unit-level coverage but lack integration tests
- Services interact with database, event bus, and KG — these interactions need verification

## Services Needing Integration Tests
1. BoardService — board CRUD, member management
2. AgentService — agent registration, permissions
3. GuidelineService — guideline CRUD, board linking
4. CommentService — comment CRUD, choice comments, responses
5. QAService — question/answer lifecycle
6. SpecService — spec CRUD, evaluation, validation gates
7. CardService — card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions
8. IdeationService — ideation lifecycle, evaluations
9. RefinementService — refinement lifecycle, spec derivation
10. SprintService — sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations", "problem_statement": "Service layer is the core business logic — needs comprehensive integration coverage. Without integration tests, regressions in service interactions go undetected until production.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "service-layer", "integration"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:33", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:56:04"}, {"id": "ac776dc2-826b-4213-ac6b-81c45d317017", "title": "Split MCP Server into Modular Tool Groups", "description": "The MCP server.py is 10,920 lines in a single file. Split into modular tool groups (boards, cards, specs, kg, etc.) for maintainability.

## Current State
- `server.py` contains all MCP tool definitions in one monolithic file
- Tools cover boards, cards, specs, ideations, refinements, KG operations, sprints, and more
- No separation of concerns between different functional areas

## Benefits of Splitting
- Each module can be tested independently
- New tools can be added without touching the main server file
- Code review becomes manageable (reviewing a 300-line module vs a 10K-line file)
- Import-time performance improves with lazy loading of unused modules", "problem_statement": "Single massive file is unmaintainable and hard to test in isolation. Any change to MCP tools requires loading the entire 10K+ line file. Testing individual tool groups is impossible without mocking everything else.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["architecture", "refactoring", "mcp"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:36:55", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:56:01"}, {"id": "ef370e92-5edf-4a18-abfb-a795764851f6", "title": "Discovery Feature Tests", "description": "Discovery intent catalog, saved searches, and search history are not tested. This is the global cross-board search feature.

## Current State
- Discovery provides cross-board semantic search via global layer
- Intent catalog defines searchable queries/templates
- Saved searches allow users to bookmark frequent searches
- Search history tracks past queries per agent

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Intent Catalog** — CRUD intent templates, execute intents
2. **Saved Searches** — create/read/update/delete saved searches
3. **Search History** — automatic recording, retrieval, pagination
4. **Cross-board ACL** — respect board permissions in search results
5. **Semantic Search** — verify embedding-based matching works
6. **Performance** — search completes within acceptable time bounds
7. **Edge Cases** — empty boards, no results, malformed queries", "problem_statement": "New feature with no automated verification. Discovery is the global cross-board search feature — critical for multi-board workflows but entirely untested.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "draft", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "discovery", "search"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:04", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:45:02"}, {"id": "d2f3f466-c5fb-4010-afed-cb7c7a65623e", "title": "Resolve Version Number Inconsistency", "description": "pyproject.toml says 0.1.5, config.py default is 0.1.4, README says 0.1.3 is current published. Single source of truth needed.

## Current State
- `okto-pulse-core/pyproject.toml`: version = "0.1.5"
- `config.py` default: version = "0.1.4"
- README: references 0.1.3 as current published version
- Three different versions across the codebase

## Impact
- Confusion during deployment and debugging
- Hard to determine actual version from running instance
- Release process doesn't enforce single source of truth", "problem_statement": "Confusion about actual version during deployment and debugging. Different parts of the system report different versions, making troubleshooting difficult.", "complexity": "small", "status": "draft", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["maintenance", "versioning"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:24", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:45:00"}, {"id": "7b4b9c8a-0296-4388-8312-2a52c11d98fd", "title": "Sprint Evaluation/Transition Gate Tests", "description": "Only basic Sprint CRUD is tested. ## Current State
- Sprint state machine: draft → active → review → closed
- Gates enforced at transitions:
  - **draft → active**: requires at least one linked card
  - **active → review**: requires scoped test coverage (all scenarios have test cards)
  - **review → closed**: requires qualitative evaluation submitted
- Basic CRUD operations are tested but gate enforcement is not

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Draft → Active** — success with cards, failure without cards
2. **Active → Review** — success with full coverage, failure with missing tests
3. **Review → Closed** — success with evaluation, failure without
4. **Gate Bypass** — verify skip flags work correctly (skip_test_coverage, etc.)
5. **Evaluation Submission** — submit sprint evaluation, verify scores and thresholds
6. **Invalid Transitions** — draft → closed should fail
7. **Cancelled State** — cancel from any state, verify no further transitions", "problem_statement": "Sprint governance rules unverified by tests. ## Current State
- Event bus publishes events to outbox table
- Workers consume with exponential backoff retry (max 5 attempts)
- Failed events after max retries go to Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)
- ConsolidationEnqueuer is the only active handler
- Retry and DLQ logic exists but has never been exercised in tests

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Happy Path** — event published, consumed successfully on first attempt
2. **Retry Success** — handler fails 2 times, succeeds on 3rd attempt
3. **DLQ Transition** — handler fails all 5 attempts, event moves to DLQ
4. **Exponential Backoff** — verify delay between retries follows pattern
5. **DLQ Recovery** — manually reprocess DLQ event
6. **Concurrent Processing** — multiple events processed in parallel
7. **Worker Crash Recovery** — worker dies mid-processing, event retried", "problem_statement": "Failure handling in event pipeline unverified. These are destructive operations that need thorough testing.

## Current State
- `archive_tree` cascades: ideation → refinements → specs → cards
- `restore_tree` reverses the cascade, restoring pre_archive_status
- Both operations set/clear the archived flag recursively
- No test file covers these operations

## Test Scenarios Needed
- Archive ideation → verify all descendants archived
- Archive refinement → verify specs and cards archived
- Archive spec → verify cards archived (not deleted)
- Restore archived tree → verify pre_archive_status restored
- Partial restore → only specified entity restored, others remain archived

- Edge case: concurrent modification during archive", "problem_statement": "Archiving an ideation cascades to refinements, specs, cards — any bug here causes data loss. - Bug cards have special fields: origin_task_id, severity, expected_behavior, observed_behavior
- Bug cards require origin_task_id and auto-resolve spec from the origin task
- Bug workflow: create → assign fix tasks → link to scenarios → resolve
- No test file covers this entire lifecycle

## Current State
- Tests exist for individual components (board CRUD, card CRUD, etc.)
- No test covers the complete lifecycle from ideation to completed task
- Governance gates exist at each stage but aren't tested together

## Pipeline Stages to Cover
1. **Ideation** — create → evaluate → move to done
2. **Refinement** — derive from ideation → scope definition → move to done
3. **Spec** — derive from refinement → add requirements → validate → promote
4. **Tasks** — create cards from spec → assign → implement → validate
5. **Sprint** — create sprint → assign tasks → gates → close
6. **Done** — verify completed workflow, conclusions recorded

## Test Structure
- Single test file: e2e_pipeline_test.py

## Current State
- Global meta-graph contains 5 node types: Decision, Entity, Criterion, Constraint, Requirement
- 6 additional node types exist but aren't in global discovery: Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode, Alternative
- Consolidation pipeline maps board-specific KG nodes to global layer
- Global discovery queries search across all boards

## Missing Node Types
1. **Bug** — bug reports linked to cards/tasks

5. **CardNode** — task cards discoverable globally

- Permission system supports: presets, agent-level overrides, granular boolean flags

- Decisions support: add, update, remove, supersede chain tracking

- Frontend uses @xyflow/react (React Flow fork) for graph visualization
- KG graph renders nodes (decisions, entities, constraints) with edges

Individual tests pass but integration between stages may have gaps that only appear in full workflows.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "e2e", "pipeline"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:50", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:10"}, {"id": "a4b017ef-8f7c-48cc-9611-ac40f44c00af", "title": "KG Global Discovery Cross-type Expansion", "description": "Expand global discovery from 5 to all 11 node types. Currently only Decision, Entity, Criterion, Constraint, Requirement are in the global layer.

## Current State
- Global meta-graph contains 5 node types: Decision, Entity, Criterion, Constraint, Requirement
- 6 additional node types exist but aren't in global discovery: Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode, Alternative
- Consolidation pipeline maps board-specific KG nodes to global layer
- Global discovery queries search across all boards

## Missing Node Types
1. **Bug** — bug reports linked to cards/tasks
2. **Learning** — lessons learned from bugs and decisions
3. **SpecNode** — spec-level knowledge for cross-board reference
4. **SprintNode** — sprint context across boards
5. **CardNode** — task cards discoverable globally
6. **Alternative** — considered alternatives from decisions

## Benefits
- Bug patterns discoverable across projects
- Learnings from one board inform work on another
- Spec references can be found regardless of which board they're on", "problem_statement": "Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode not discoverable across boards. Global discovery is incomplete — users can't find relevant context that exists in other boards.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["knowledge-graph", "discovery", "architecture"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:23", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:07"}, {"id": "ff67518b-88ba-44b2-8fb1-fc2b3e13b0ed", "title": "Permission System Tests", "description": "Permission presets, overrides, and granular flags are not tested. The Agent model has permission flags that control MCP tool access.

## Current State
- Permission system supports: presets, agent-level overrides, granular boolean flags
- Agents have per-tool permissions controlling MCP access
- Presets define default permission sets (e.g., "full_access", "read_only")
- Overrides allow customizing specific permissions per agent

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Preset CRUD** — create/read/update/delete permission presets
2. **Agent Override** — apply override to agent, verify tool access changes
3. **Granular Flags** — toggle individual flags, verify enforcement at MCP layer
4. **Inheritance** — agent inherits from preset, override specific tools
5. **Default Permissions** — new agents get correct defaults
6. **MCP Tool Enforcement** — denied tools return proper errors
7. **Admin Bypass** — admin agents bypass all restrictions", "problem_statement": "Security-critical feature without test coverage. Permission system controls which agents can access which tools — gaps here could allow unauthorized operations.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "security", "permissions"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:56", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:04"}, {"id": "3cc8bde5-3fbc-48dc-a6b4-e72a5e3c3cf8", "title": "Implement Decision Management Tests", "description": "Decision CRUD (add/update/remove/supersede) operations are not explicitly tested despite being core spec features.

## Current State
- Decisions support: add, update, remove, supersede chain tracking
- Decisions link to functional requirements and task cards
- Decisions feed into KG consolidation pipeline as Decision nodes
- Supersession chains tracked with automatic status management
- No test file covers this lifecycle

## Test Scenarios Needed
- Add decision with all fields
- Update decision fields
- Remove (soft-delete/revoked) decision
- Supersede chain: A → B → C, verify active tracking
- Link decisions to requirements and tasks
- Coverage gate enforcement
- KG consolidation of decisions", "problem_statement": "Decisions drive the Knowledge Graph consolidation pipeline — untested code path. Any regression in decision management could silently corrupt the KG.", "complexity": "large", "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "decisions", "knowledge-graph"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:17", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:44:01"}, {"id": "e494f277-c913-4803-ac8c-0fd446376978", "title": "React Flow v12 Migration for KG Graph UI", "description": "The frontend uses @xyflow/react for KG graph visualization. Migrate to React Flow v12 for improved performance and features.

## Current State
- Frontend uses @xyflow/react (React Flow fork) for graph visualization
- KG graph renders nodes (decisions, entities, constraints) with edges
- d3-force integration for layout computation
- Current version may have limitations for large graphs (100+ nodes)

## Migration Considerations
- React Flow v12 has improved virtual rendering for large graphs
- Better TypeScript support and type safety
- Performance improvements in edge rendering
- Updated node/edge component APIs
- Breaking changes in some component props

## Components Affected
1. KGGraph — main graph container
2. KGNode — custom node components for each node type
3. KGLegend — legend showing node type colors
4. KGSearch — search/filter overlay
5. MiniMap — zoom overview", "problem_statement": "Current version may have limitations for large graph rendering. As boards grow, the KG graph becomes slow and unresponsive with many nodes and edges.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "evaluating", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["frontend", "knowledge-graph", "performance"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:38:40", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:43:45"}, {"id": "113867cd-bc37-4413-bb6e-d418859c597b", "title": "Frontend Component Test Coverage Expansion", "description": "Vitest setup exists but frontend component tests likely have minimal coverage. Key components (kanban board, spec editor, KG graph) need tests.

## Current State
- Vitest configured with jsdom environment
- Test setup at `src/test/setup.ts`
- Frontend is a React SPA embedded in the community edition
- Components: kanban board, spec editor, KG graph visualization, ideation flow

## Components Needing Tests
1. **KanbanBoard** — drag-drop card movement, status columns
2. **SpecEditor** — requirement editing, acceptance criteria, test scenarios
3. **KGGraph** — node rendering, edge display, zoom/pan
4. **IdeationFlow** — ideation creation, evaluation form
5. **SprintView** — sprint cards, progress tracking
6. **QABoard** — question/answer interactions", "problem_statement": "UI regressions not caught by automated tests. Frontend changes can break critical workflows without any test failure.", "complexity": "medium", "status": "evaluating", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "frontend", "coverage"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:37:41", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T01:43:02"}]}

{"board_id": "a5010bac-4cf7-43b7-a275-878b4759b83a", "total": 5, "offset": 0, "limit": 200, "specs": [{"id": "fea52138-d18d-4079-b3ed-7204712e2470", "title": "Agent Permission Enforcement Test Suite", "description": "Security-critical test suite covering the full permission system including preset CRUD, agent-level overrides, and granular flag enforcement at the MCP tool layer. This refinement ensures that permission controls are correctly enforced and that unauthorized operations are properly blocked.

Key test areas:
- **Preset CRUD**: Create/read/update/delete permission presets (full_access, read_only, custom)
- **Agent Override**: Apply override to agent, verify tool access changes immediately
- **Granular Flags**: Toggle individual boolean flags, verify enforcement at MCP layer
- **Inheritance**: Agent inherits from preset, override specific tools, verify precedence
- **Default Permissions**: New agents receive correct default permissions
- **MCP Tool Enforcement**: Denied tools return proper error responses (403/permission_denied)
- **Admin Bypass**: Admin agents bypass all restrictions; non-admin cannot bypass", "status": "approved", "version": 2, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "security", "permissions"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:07:04", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:48:28"}, {"id": "460ee29c-7880-4693-b84f-202c54fbec4c", "title": "Decision CRUD Lifecycle Test Suite", "description": "Comprehensive test suite covering the full lifecycle of decision management operations. This refinement focuses on testing add_decision, update_decision, remove_decision, supersede chain tracking, and linked_requirements/task associations. The tests verify that decisions correctly transition through active → superseded → revoked states and that the supersession chain is maintained accurately.

Key test areas:
- **Add Decision**: All fields including title, rationale, context, alternatives_considered, linked_requirements, notes
- **Update Decision**: Partial updates, full updates, CLEAR operations on optional fields
- **Remove Decision**: Soft-delete to revoked status, history preservation
- **Supersede Chain**: A → B → C chain with automatic status management and active tracking
- **Linked Requirements**: Pipe-separated FR index resolution and validation
- **Coverage Gate**: Opt-in coverage gate enforcement for decisions", "status": "approved", "version": 2, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "decisions", "lifecycle"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:41", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:48:18"}, {"id": "e20a5c1a-319b-4569-a8d7-655ee94e0504", "title": "Full Pipeline E2E Test Orchestration", "description": "Umbrella E2E validation suite exercising the complete pipeline: ideation → refinement → spec → tasks → sprint → done. This test suite verifies governance gates, status transitions, and data integrity across all stages of the workflow.

Key test scenarios:
1. **Golden Path**: Full lifecycle from ideation creation through task completion with conclusion recording
2. **Bug Workflow**: Bug card creation linked to origin task, severity tracking, resolution verification
3. **Decision Supersession**: Decision chain A→B→C within spec context, coverage gate enforcement
4. **Sprint Gates**: Sprint creation → task assignment → test coverage gate → evaluation → closure

Test structure uses async test fixtures with temp database (from conftest), marked with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective execution in CI. Tests run on main branch merges only, not on every PR.", "status": "in_progress", "version": 8, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "e2e", "pipeline"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:45", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:39:48"}, {"id": "cc08b776-cd18-455e-b9c1-2777509aab24", "title": "Global Meta-Graph Node Type Expansion", "description": "Extend the global discovery layer from 5 to all 11 node types, enabling cross-board discovery of Bug, Learning, SpecNode, SprintNode, CardNode, and Alternative nodes. This refinement implements the consolidation pipeline extensions required for each new node type.

Key implementation areas:
- **Bug Nodes**: Bug reports linked to cards/tasks, discoverable across boards with severity metadata
- **Learning Nodes**: Lessons learned from bugs and decisions, connected via validates relationship
- **SpecNode**: Spec-level knowledge for cross-board reference and dependency tracking
- **SprintNode**: Sprint context across boards for velocity and planning insights
- **CardNode**: Task cards discoverable globally for cross-project task correlation
- **Alternative Nodes**: Considered alternatives from decisions, including reason_discarded

Each node type requires: CREATE NODE TABLE in global meta-graph schema, consolidation pipeline mapping, edge type definitions for relationships, ACL enforcement across board boundaries, and discovery query updates.", "status": "in_progress", "version": 8, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["knowledge-graph", "discovery", "architecture"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:43", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:39:45"}, {"id": "477d82be-ec97-4261-a796-c35751af0cf8", "title": "Service Integration Test Framework", "description": "Comprehensive integration test framework covering all 18 service classes with CRUD operations, error paths, and edge cases. This refinement defines the test structure, fixture strategy, and coverage targets for service-level integration testing.

Key test areas:
- **BoardService**: Board CRUD, member management, guideline linking
- **AgentService**: Agent registration, permissions, preset assignment
- **GuidelineService**: Guideline CRUD, board linking, tag filtering
- **CommentService**: Comment CRUD, choice comments, response tracking
- **QAService**: Question/answer lifecycle, choice questions, deletion
- **SpecService**: Spec CRUD, evaluation submission, validation gates
- **CardService**: Card CRUD, dependencies, status transitions, conclusions
- **IdeationService**: Ideation lifecycle, evaluations, complexity assessment
- **RefinementService**: Refinement lifecycle, spec derivation, scope management
- **SprintService**: Sprint CRUD, gates, evaluations, task assignment

Uses shared test database fixture with per-test reset from conftest. Tests real service-to-service dependencies while mocking external dependencies (KG, event bus).", "status": "in_progress", "version": 8, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "service-layer", "integration"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:06:48", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:28:12"}]}

Use pytest fixtures for setup per the spec decisions.", "status": "in_progress", "priority": "high", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "460ee29c-7880-4693-b84f-202c54fbec4c", "test_scenario_ids": ["ts_acc86a2b"], "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "07766f8b-b8f0-457c-9a1f-d0b339e52c86", "title": "Implement remove_decision and supersede chain tests", "description": "Test soft-delete to revoked status via remove_decision and verify history preservation. Verify full chain depth tracking and current_active identification using real database (per Q&A).", "status": "in_progress", "priority": "high", "position": 0, "assignee_id": null, "spec_id": "460ee29c-7880-4693-b84f-202c54fbec4c", "test_scenario_ids": null, "due_date": null, "labels": []}, {"id": "d02abf41-51d7-4c2c-aaea-1e06602d2040", "title": "Add SpecNode and SprintNode to global meta-graph", "description": "Create node tables for SpecNode (title, description, status, board_id) and SprintNode (title, objective, expected_outcome, status, spec_ref). This refinement focuses on testing add_decision, update_decision, remove_decision, supersede chain tracking, and linked_requirements/task associations. The tests verify that decisions correctly transition through active → superseded → revoked states and that the supersession chain is maintained accurately.

Key test areas:
- **Add Decision**: All fields including title, rationale, context, alternatives_considered, linked_requirements, notes
- **Update Decision**: Partial updates, full updates, CLEAR operations on optional fields
- **Remove Decision**: Soft-delete to revoked status, history preservation
- **Supersede Chain**: A → B → C chain with automatic status management and active tracking
- **Linked Requirements**: Pipe-separated FR index resolution and validation
- **Coverage Gate**: Opt-in coverage gate enforcement for decisions", "in_scope": ["add_decision", "update_decision", "remove_decision", "supersede_decision", "linked_requirements", "coverage_gate_enforcement", "decision_status_transitions"], "out_of_scope": ["KG_consolidation_pipeline", "global_discovery", "semantic_search_quality", "decision_vector_embedding"], "status": "done", "version": 1, "assignee_id": null, "labels": ["testing", "decisions", "lifecycle"], "created_at": "2026-04-28T01:56:16", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:05:29"}]}

2. **Bug Workflow**: Bug card creation linked to origin task, severity tracking, resolution verification
3. **Decision Supersession**: Decision chain A→B→C within spec context, coverage gate enforcement
4. **Sprint Gates**: Sprint creation → task assignment → test coverage gate → evaluation → closure

Test structure uses async test fixtures with temp database (from conftest), marked with @pytest.mark.e2e for selective execution in CI.
